## Fuente: .\docs-md-py\en-us\unreal-engine\unreal-editor-interface.md

# Unreal Editor Interface

> Source: https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/en-us/unreal-engine/unreal-editor-interface

> Application Version: 5.7

This introduction to the **Unreal Engine** **interface** describes the most common **buttons**, **panels**, and **toolbars** you’ll interact with in the **Unreal** **Editor**. In this page, you’ll learn more about what they do and how you can use them. While some of these elements are generally the same across various parts of the engine, you should spend some time getting familiar with their general purpose and functionality, especially if you are new to developing projects with Unreal Engine. You can also follow links throughout this page for a deeper dive into how you can use them.

When you open the engine for the first time, the **Level Editor** opens. This provides the core creation functionality to the Unreal Editor and is used for designing and constructing levels and environments. It is where you’ll spend most of your time developing content for your project. There are different editors in Unreal Engine used for different purposes.

Once your project opens, you will see the following layout, keeping in mind that what’s in the level will differ depending on the project you open. For this example, we are using the First Person template.

![Image](https://dev.epicgames.com/community/api/documentation/image/669481b3-c96f-4e63-9c89-2f8025e8210f)

| Number | Name | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | Menu Bar | Uses these menus to access common application actions, like saving, and creating new levels. You’ll also find options for opening editor windows and tools that are useful for specific functions like debugging and more. |
| 2 | Main Toolbar | Contains shortcuts for some of the most common tools and editors in Unreal Engine, as well as shortcuts to enter Play mode (run your game inside Unreal Editor) and to deploy your project to other platforms. |
| 3 | Viewport Toolbar | Includes common tools used to manipulate objects in the level by moving, rotating, and scaling them, as well as snapping tools for moving objects along a grid, rotation angle, or scaling amount. It also includes perspective and orthographic views along with debugging and visualization view mode and other settings you can use while working in the viewport. |
| 4 | Level Viewport | Displays the contents of your Level, such as Cameras, Actors, Static Meshes, and so on. |
| 5 | Outliner | Displays a hierarchical tree view of all content in your Level. |
| 6 | Details panel | Appears when you select an Actor. Displays various properties for that Actor, such as its **Transform** (position in the Level), Static Mesh, Material, and physics settings. This panel displays different settings depending on what you select in the Level Viewport. |
| 7 | Content Drawer | Opens the Content Drawer, a temporary Content Browser window, from which you can access all of the Assets in your Project. |
| 8 | Bottom Toolbar | Contains shortcuts to the Command Console, Output Log, and Derived Data functionality. Also displays source control status. |

## Menu Bar

Each editor in Unreal Engine has a **menu bar** that is located in the top-left of the editor window (Windows), or at the top-left of the display (macOS). Some of the menus, such as **File**, **Window**, and **Help**, are present in all editor windows, not just the Level Editor. Some other menus are specific to their own editors.

![Image](https://dev.epicgames.com/community/api/documentation/image/f4b51aec-4b51-478e-8ca8-a5bb81c8d3bc)

## Main Toolbar

The **Main Toolbar** contains shortcuts to some of the most used tools and commands in Unreal Editor. It is divided into the following areas:

![Image](https://dev.epicgames.com/community/api/documentation/image/96d4b7e3-0822-4752-b8e4-1eedf68053a1)

| Number | Name | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | Save button | Click this button to save the level that is currently open. |
| 2 | Select the open level | Click this button to show the currently loaded level in the Content Browser. This will open the most recently used Content Browser, which can be the docked Content Drawer or standalone Content Browser panel. |
| 3 | Mode selection | Contains shortcuts for quickly switching between different modes to edit content within your level. These modes also change the primary behavior of the Level Editor, which can include toolbar options designed for each mode specifically. You can click the links to go to their respective pages to learn more about a mode. [Selection](https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/en-us/unreal-engine/select-mode-in-unreal-engine) Landscape [Foliage](https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/en-us/unreal-engine/foliage-mode-in-unreal-engine) [Mesh Paint](https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/en-us/unreal-engine/mesh-paint-mode-in-unreal-engine) [Modeling](https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/en-us/unreal-engine/modeling-mode-quick-start-in-unreal-engine) [Fracture](https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/en-us/unreal-engine/fracture-mode-selection-tools-user-guide) [Brush](https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/en-us/unreal-engine/geometry-brush-actors-in-unreal-engine) [Animation](https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/en-us/unreal-engine/animation-editor-mode-in-unreal-engine) |
| 4 | Content shortcuts | Contains shortcuts for adding and opening common types of content within the Level Editor. The **Create**button can be used to choose from a list of common **Actor Types** and **Recently Used** assets from the Content Browser to quickly add to your level. The **Blueprints** button can be used to create and access blueprints. The **Cinematics** button can be used to create and access a **Level Sequence** or **Master Sequence** cinematic. |
| 5 | Play mode controls | Contains the shortcut buttons Play, Skip, Stop, and Eject for running your game in the editor. |
| 6 | Platforms menu | Contains a series of options you can use to configure, prepare, and deploy your project to different platforms, such as desktop platforms such as Windows, macOS and Linux, mobile, and consoles. |

## Viewport Toolbar

The Viewport Toolbar can be used to accomplish a number of tasks when building levels. It is located at the top of the Level Viewport, and its settings and tools are grouped into the following categories:

![Image](https://dev.epicgames.com/community/api/documentation/image/55cc5842-0874-4d8d-84fc-0f3fc2988797)

| Number | Name | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | Transform and Snapping tools | These are the options to swap between different Transform tools and change the snapping options. |
| 2 | Camera tools | These are the options to switch between Perspective and Orthographic views and change camera movement speed. |
| 3 | View Mode and Show Flag options | These are the options to pick between different view modes like Lit, Unlit, and Wireframe, and show flags related to the current viewport to hide and reveal types of content in the Level Viewport. |
| 4 | Performance and Scalability tools | This dropdown can be used to reveal settings like Viewport Scalability and Material Quality Level. |
| 5 | Viewport options | These options can be used to change viewport related settings, like mouse sensitivity in the Level Viewport. |

To learn more about this toolbar, read the [Viewport Toolbar](https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/en-us/unreal-engine/viewport-toolbar) documentation.

## Level Viewport

The **Level** **Viewport** displays the contents of the level that is currently loaded. When opening a project in Unreal Engine, the project's default level is loaded For example, if you open the First or Third Person Template project, you’ll see a gray box area (like the one pictured below) with some physics objects, floors, walls, and ramps.

The Level Viewport is where you can view and edit the contents of your Level, whether it's a game environment, a product visualization, or something else.

The Level Viewport can generally display the contents of the Level in two different ways:

- Perspective is a 3D view you can navigate to see the contents of the viewport from different angles.
- Orthographic is a 2D view that looks towards a specific direction (top, down, left, right, front, back). This creates a view where the distance from the object doesn’t affect size or provide a sense of depth.

| Perspective view | Top view | Right side view |
| --- | --- | --- |
| ![Image](https://dev.epicgames.com/community/api/documentation/image/eafa8ca5-da06-4c39-8914-6e81cc0573f1) | ![Image](https://dev.epicgames.com/community/api/documentation/image/8ab4d964-b22f-42f6-8829-b9ea33ab99a3) | ![Image](https://dev.epicgames.com/community/api/documentation/image/7b4be143-d77b-4612-8292-0b2e87ddf8e3) |

The **Viewport Type** can be changed between **Default** **Viewport** and **Cinematic Viewport**. Default Viewport displays gizmos and icons in the level like Camera actors, Collision Components, and the World Grid.

Additionally, you can change the **View Mode** of your viewport by pressing the Lit button in the top-right corner of the viewport. Lit is the default view mode used for real-time applications in the Perspective view, where you can preview your scene with normally rendered lighting. If you select another View Mode, this button will be titled after that. So, if you have already selected one of the Orthographic views (Top, Front, Left, and so on), the button might say **Wireframe** in your viewport as that is the default View Mode for the Orthographic view. If you press it, and select Lit, you will see your environment fully lit again in any view mode.

[Video: V_OmFAlz](https://dev.epicgames.com/community/api/cms/videos/V_OmFAlz/embed.html)

You can use the **Scalability** options to change the visual fidelity of which your level is being previewed. By clicking the **Performance and Scalability** button and clicking **Viewport Scalability**, you can change to a different scalability group.

[Video: V_fen0tO](https://dev.epicgames.com/community/api/cms/videos/V_fen0tO/embed.html)

| Low option | High option | Cinematic option |
| --- | --- | --- |
| ![Image](https://dev.epicgames.com/community/api/documentation/image/ef04c505-63e7-4174-b53b-1fbde47be74b) | ![Image](https://dev.epicgames.com/community/api/documentation/image/e5deea9c-e839-4b0b-8228-3f910ba3b14a) | ![Image](https://dev.epicgames.com/community/api/documentation/image/c7187af5-c647-44f2-be29-5780927c44a6) |

To learn more about the scalability options, see the Scalability Reference page.

## Navigating the Viewport

Let’s explore your Level! To control the viewport camera, the perspective through which you view the Level, press and hold the Right Mouse Button (RMB) anywhere in the viewport. While holding down RMB, you can use the **W**, **A**, **S**, and **D** keys to fly through the scene. You can fly up and down with the **E** and **Q** keys.

Releasing RMB and holding down the Left Mouse Button (LMB) can be used along with dragging the mouse to move the camera forward, backward, and rotate it left or right. This provides an alternative method for navigating the environment with greater precision.

| Navigating with LMB | Navigating with RMB |
| --- | --- |
| [Video: V_C82Mti](https://dev.epicgames.com/community/api/cms/videos/V_C82Mti/embed.html) | [Video: V_DHHSmZ](https://dev.epicgames.com/community/api/cms/videos/V_DHHSmZ/embed.html) |

Clicking on an object in the Level with the LMB **selects** the object. Selected objects can be manipulated using the **Transform Tool gizmos** in the viewport. Gizmos are a set of on-screen handles used to move, rotate, or scale the object along specific axes, activated based on the transform tool you select. You can activate the **Translate** tool by pressing **W**, the **Rotation** tool by pressing **E**, and the **Scale** tool by pressing **R**. Alternatively, you can use the Toolbar located at the top-right of the Viewport to switch between Transform tools.

Once you have selected a tool, you can manipulate the object using the gizmo’s axes. With the Translate or Scale tools, you can also drag from the center of the gizmo to adjust all axes simultaneously. This is particularly useful for uniformly scaling an object without disproportionately affecting any single axis.

[Video: V_6Pmoqm](https://dev.epicgames.com/community/api/cms/videos/V_6Pmoqm/embed.html)

The viewport will have snapping enabled by default for every way of transforming your object. You can turn these off or change their snapping values by using the individual settings on the top-left side of the viewport.

![Image](https://dev.epicgames.com/community/api/documentation/image/a3453a28-9e0f-4e40-af53-501650f07c5e)

For more information on how to navigate a level, see the [Viewport Controls](https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/en-us/unreal-engine/viewport-controls-in-unreal-engine) page.

## Content Drawer and Content Browser

The **Content Browser** is a file explorer for your project where you can organize all the assets that make up your project. These assets can include Blueprints, Geometry (static and skeletal), textures, materials, and so on.

The **Content Drawer**, located in the bottom-left corner of the Unreal Editor, opens a special instance of the Content Browser that automatically minimizes when it loses focus (that is, when you click away from it). To keep it open, click the **Dock in Layout** button in the top-right corner of the Content Drawer. This creates a new instance of the Content Browser, but you can still open a new Content Drawer. You can also open a Content Drawer by holding down **CTRL** keyand pressing the **Space** key.

[Video: V_gcypZW](https://dev.epicgames.com/community/api/cms/videos/V_gcypZW/embed.html)

## Bottom Toolbar

The **Bottom Toolbar** contains shortcuts and settings to various elements of the Unreal Editor. Depending on the Editor you are in (like the Viewport Editor), some of these options might not be available.

![Image](https://dev.epicgames.com/community/api/documentation/image/bda8f673-5f85-48e1-b434-527727894e44)

| Number | Name | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | Content Drawer | Temporary content browser window which gets dismissed as it loses focus, used for quick access. |
| 2 | OutputLog Drawer and Command Prompt | Opening the Output Log and entering Console Commands, including changing between executing Unreal Commands and Python scripts. |
| 3 | Tracing | Start tracing, save a Snapshot of the Current Trace Buffer, and change Trace settings. |
| 4 | Stall Log | Opens the Stall Log. |
| 5 | Derived Data | Provides [Derived Data](https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/en-us/unreal-engine/derived-data-settings-in-the-unreal-engine-project-settings) functionality. |
| 6 | Live Coding Compiler | Recompile and reload C++ code on the fly using Live Coding. |
| 7 | Save Status | View the number of Levels with changes not yet saved. You can click on this option to view unsaved Levels. |
| 8 | Revision Control | Connect to Revision Control and View Changes, Submit Content, or Check Out Modified Files. |

## Outliner

The **Outliner** panel displays a hierarchical view of all content in your level. It is located in the upper-right corner of the Unreal Editor window. You can have up to four different Outliners, each with its own column layout and filter configuration.

![Image](https://dev.epicgames.com/community/api/documentation/image/46e3571a-9cb5-423f-84a6-6a904a743209)

You can hide or reveal objects by clicking their associated **Eye** button. Accessing an object’s **context menu** can be done by right-clicking that object in the Outliner. You can then perform additional, actor-specific operations from that menu.

To keep your levels organized, you can create, move, and delete content folders. Folders can be used to group objects in your level together.

## Details Panel

When you select an actor in the Level Viewport or the Outliner, the **Details** panel will show the settings and properties that affect the actor you selected. By default, it is located on the right side of the Unreal Editor window, under the Outliner panel.

![Image](https://dev.epicgames.com/community/api/documentation/image/491f8e6d-38cd-4973-8661-13dd3d5fe7bf)

As shown in the image above:

| Number | Description |
| --- | --- |
| 1 | The ramp object in the Level is highlighted after being selected (clicked on). |
| 2 | The ramp object is also highlighted in the **Outliner** panel automatically. Selecting an object through the Level Viewport or the Outliner Panel highlights it the same. |
| 3 | The **Details** panel shows the components and properties of the ramp object. You can modify the properties of an object through the Details panel. To learn more about components, see the [Components Window](https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/en-us/unreal-engine/components-window-in-unreal-engine) page. |

You can filter the actor’s properties to view and change by selecting a specific Component at the top of the Details panel. You can lock the current selection into the Details panel, which you can use to highlight other objects while keeping the Details panel locked on the first object.

---

## Fuente: .\docs-md-py\en-us\unreal-engine\unreal-editor-preferences.md

# Editor Preferences

> Source: https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/en-us/unreal-engine/unreal-editor-preferences

> Application Version: 5.7

![Editor Preferences Dialog](https://d1iv7db44yhgxn.cloudfront.net/documentation/images/8b07feba-2287-414c-ade0-30ed6a392436/editorpreferences.png)


The **Editor Preferences** window is used to modify settings that control the behavior of Unreal Editor with respect to controls, viewports, source control, auto-saving, and more.

To open the **Editor Preferences** window, click **Edit > Editor Preferences**.

## Level Editor Preferences - Viewports

### Controls

| Option | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Flight Camera Control type** | This setting determines whether the flight camera is used and how it is accessed. The flight camera uses the W, A, S, and D keys to dolly and pan the camera, similar to how standard FPS controls work. W and S dolly the camera in and out, while A and D pan, or strafe, the camera side to side. When active, this overrides any hotkeys using these controls, including toggling show flags.   * **Use WASD for Camera Controls**: The flight camera and WASD controls are always active. * **Use WASD Only When Right Mouse Button is Held**: The flight camera and WASD controls are only active when the RMB is pressed. * **Never Use WASD for Camera Controls**: The flight camera and WASD controls are never active. |
| **Grab and Drag to Move Orthographic Cameras** | If enabled, clicking the Left or Right Mouse Buttons and dragging the mouse in an orthographic viewport will scroll the camera. Essentially what this means in practice is when you click and drag, the scene will move with the mouse. With this disabled, the scene moves opposite of the mouse. |
| **Orthographic Zoom to Cursor Position** | If enabled, zooming in the orthographic viewports will be centered around the mouse cursor. When disabled, zooming is centered on the center of the viewport. |
| **Link Orthographic Viewport Movement** | If enabled, all orthographic viewports will be linked to focus on the same position. So moving the camera in one orthographic viewport will cause the other orthographic viewports to follow. When disabled, all viewports can be navigated independently. |
| **Aspect Ratio Axis Constraint** | Determines which axis of the perspective viewport is used to control the field of view (FOV) to maintain the aspect ratio when resizing.   * **Maintain Y-Axis FOV**: The Y-Axis (vertical) is used to determine the FOV. * **Maintain X-Axis FOV**: The X-Axis (horizontal) is used to determine the FOV. * **Maintain Major Axis FOV**: The larger of the two axes is used to determine the FOV. |
| **Mouse Scroll Camera Speed** | Determines the speed at which the perspective camera moves when navigating with the mouse. |

### Viewport Look and Feel

| Option | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Highlight Objects Under Mouse Cursor** | If enabled, objects will be highlighted using a transparent overlay in viewports while hovering over them with the mouse cursor. |

### Editor Look and Feel

| Option | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Use Small Tool Bar Icons** | If enabled, toolbar icons across the whole editor will be smaller and without labels. [See below for details.](https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/en-us/unreal-engine/unreal-editor-preferences#smalltoolbaricons) |

#### Small Tool Bar Icons

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  |  |
| **Normal** | **Small** |

### Level Editing

| Option | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Enable Combined Translate/Rotate Widget** | If enabled, the combined Translation and Rotate-Z tool is made available. |
| **Clicking BSP Enables Brush** | If enabled, clicking on a Brush surface selects the brush and **Ctrl + Shift + Click** selects the surface. When disabled, the opposite is true; clicking selects the surface and **Ctrl + Shift + Click** selects the brush. |
| **Update BSP Automatically** | If enabled, Brush geometry rebuilds automatically when brushes are moved or modified in the viewport. When disabled, geometry must be rebuilt to see the changes. |
| **Preserve Actor Scale on Replace** | If enabled, replacing Actors will respect the scale of the original Actor. Otherwise, the replaced Actors will have a scale of 1.0. |

### Source Control

| Option | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Prompt for Checkout on Package Modification** | If enabled, the editor will automatically prompt for SCC checkout on package modification. |
| **Allow Source Control** | If enabled, the editor source control integration for packages and maps is used. |
| **Add New Files when Modified** | If enabled, new files are automatically added to source control. |
| **Host** | The host and port for your source control server. Example: `hostname:1234` |
| **Username** | The user name to sue when connecting to source control. |
| **Workspace** | The clientspec for source control. |

### Particles

| Option | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Use Curves for Distributions** | If enabled, Distributions will use the curves in place of the baked lookup tables when being rendered in the editor. |

### Content Browser

| Option | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Auto Reimport Textures** | If enabled, source files for textures are monitored and textures are automatically re-imported when the source file is modified. |
| **Assets to Load at Once before Warning** | The number of objects to load at once in the **Content Browser** before displaying a warning about loading many assets. |

### Privacy

The Editor in Unreal Engine 4 (UE4) collects usage data to help Epic continually improve UE4. There are three types of data that the Editor can collect:

* Crash reports - generated when an error causes the Engine to crash.
* Bug reports (also known as ensures) - generated when an issue causes the project to reach a certain unforeseen state, but does not cause a crash.
* Usage data (also known as analytics) - a collection of data about the user.

When the Engine crashes, a crash report is generated. Crash reports automatically offer you a choice of whether or not to send crash data to Epic.

#### Disable Bug Reports

For bug reports, you can disable data collection in the **Privacy** category of Editor Preferences. Follow these steps to disable bug reports.

1. In the menu bar, click **Edit > Editor Preferences**.
2. In the Editor Preferences dialog, scroll down to the **Privacy** category.
3. Click **Bug Reports**. In the right pane, under **Options**, the default setting is **Send unattended bug reports**.
4. If you do not want bug reports sent to Epic, click **Don't Send**.

#### Disable Usage Data

Usage data primarily consists of aggregated or anonymized information that does not directly identify you as an individual user. However, this usage data may also include the following:

* User's IP address
* User's Internet Service Provider (ISP)
* User's Continent, Country and City
* User's Epic Account ID
* User's project name

If you are concerned about privacy and want to opt out of usage data collection, you can change the setting in the **Privacy** category of Editor Preferences. Follow these steps to disable usage data collection.

1. In the **Editor Preferences** dialog, scroll down to the **Privacy** category.
2. Click **Usage Data**. In the right pane, under **Options**, the default setting is **Send Usage Data**.
3. If you do not want usage data sent to Epic, click **Don't Send**.

To learn more about Epic's general privacy practices, see Epic's [Privacy Policy](https://www.epicgames.com/site/en-US/privacypolicy).

### Auto Save

| Option | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Enable AutoSave** | If enabled, the editor will automatically perform a save operation at specified intervals. |
| **Save Maps** | If enabled, the auto-save procedure will attempt to save modified, loaded maps. |
| **Save Packages** | If enabled, the auto-save procedure will attempt to save modified content packages. |
| **Frequency in Minutes** | The time interval in minutes to wait between auto-saves. |
| **Warning in Seconds** | The number of seconds the warning is displayed before an auto-save is performed. |

# Unreal Engine 5.7 Release Notes

> Source: https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/en-us/unreal-engine/unreal-engine-5-7-release-notes

> Application Version: 5.7

## What's New?

The release of Unreal Engine 5.7 brings further improvements to the UE5 toolset. This release delivers improvements in a wide variety of areas, including Rendering, Character and Animation, Worldbuilding, PCG, and more.

This release includes improvements submitted by our community of Unreal Engine developers on GitHub. Thank you to each of these contributors to Unreal Engine 5.7:

AlexanderVeselov-arm, AlexBlackfrost, Almax27, andreibazi, az6667, Bit-Rot, Brian-PD, brpendle-ti, bturner, chrysos8201, ckendal3, ColinGulliver, CookiePLMonster, Darcy3000s, dasherman-ea, davidbaack, dmergele, DoubleDeez, DrewWeth, DTG-Will-Marshall, dyanikoglu, erebel55, Eric2-Praxinos, Erlandys, faisa-believer, foobit, gamethread, geordiemhall, glecko, GreenLightning, guansdu, H3mul, IngSubstance, iniside, jamesfAnet, jamespark-unreal, JasonCoombs, jdamdami, jerobarraco, jeysym, Jiboo, JohnJFarrow, Jordonbc, jorge-axiomvfx, jorgenpt, jpritchard1010, KaosSpectrum, KarimLUCCIN, KeithRare, kimkun07-k, kissSimple, klukule, KXOC, laurencei, lclara-BARB, LHG-JonAnderJimenez, ligazetom, LightKL, lsandersljungberg, LucaFranzo98, Maigo, MarcusSvensson92, Mario-Azcona, MartinWickhamFB, Mattiwatti, melku, michael-buschbeck-ms, Minus4-44, moumee, nickdarnell, phochstrasser, PICO-XR, QRare, r457-Ao2, rdunnington, reduf, redxdev, RiotJDebern, rohyunsang, sareali, serhanio, SilverWinter0511, slonopotamus, SoulSharer, sportbikerr1, ssutherland-riot, SukenderDontNod, Sythenz, teddemunnik, tioez326, tokyocplusplus, Tt-Wes, tustanivsky, Vaei, vladbelousoff, vogoltsov, WangKan, Wizzard033, wmiller, x157, yangskyboxlabs, yoonbigbear, zachlute-arenanet, ZeroEightSix, ZeroErrors, zhou-

```json
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false

    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "<b>Nanite Foliage</b> is an industry-leading rendering path, which at its core is comprised of three integral systems:",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }

    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<b>Nanite Assemblies</b>, which manage the foliage part instances into one cohesive unit.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<b>Nanite Skinning</b>, which determines the dynamic behavior it will have with systems like wind.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<b>Nanite Voxels</b>, which are pixel sized voxels that are able to retain details, animation and material characteristics based on the distance from the camera, all at a fraction of the original cost.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "When combined, this allows for more robust worlds to be imagined with lifelike and animated foliage that can be delivered within a 60fps budget on current generation hardware.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "New Features:",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Nanite Assemblies, for building foliage assets using instanced parts to keep asset sizes small.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          },
          {
            "type": "enhanced_list",
            "style": "unordered",
            "items": [
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "Blueprint API for building Nanite Assemblies.",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ],
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "As well as in-editor tooling to facilitate Nanite Assembly creation from selections.",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ],
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "Importer support for USD. Exposed USD schemas to use in DCC tools to mark up scenes to import as assemblies.",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ]
            ],
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Skeletal Mesh-driven dynamic wind; trees are skeletal meshes and the new Dynamic Wind plugin enables procedural wind to affect their bones.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Nanite Voxels, for efficiently rendering foliage geometry in the distance.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "TSR Thin Geometry detection to better handle foliage.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "SMAA (Experimental)",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "Mobile and desktop renderers now support <b>Subpixel Morphological Anti-Aliasing (SMAA)</b>. You can enable it with the mobile renderer using the CVar <code class=\"inline-code\">r.Mobile.AntiAliasing=5</code>, and with the desktop renderer using the CVar \n<code class=\"inline-code\">r.AntiAliasingMethod=5</code>.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "This feature improves visual fidelity by providing high-quality edge smoothing with minimal performance impact, it is an efficient technique for mobile games.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "SMAA anti-aliasing is enabled on all platforms.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Adjust quality settings using <code class=\"inline-code\">r.SMAA.Quality</code>",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Adjust edge detection between color or luminance using <code class=\"inline-code\">r.SMAA.EdgeMode</code>",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Improve edge smoothness without heavy GPU cost.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "Currently an experimental feature. The quality settings provide for tuning tradeoffs between performance or visual fidelity.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    }
  ],
  "excerpt_hash_id": "WYvO",
  "settings": {
    "is_hidden": false
  }
}
```

## Character and Animation

```json
{
  "type": "include",
  "excerpt_id": 2519,
  "excerpt_assignment_id": 2048,
  "blocks": [
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Motion Matching Chooser Integration (Experimental)",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "video",
      "video_id": "V_0ZU7uc",
      "provider": "kaltura",
      "caption": "",
      "autoplay": false,
      "loop": false,
      "width": null,
      "video": {
        "height": 643,
        "width": 1480,
        "identifier": "V_0ZU7uc"
      },
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "In UE 5.7, we've brought our first iteration of <b>Motion Matching</b> integrated into <b>Choosers</b>! We're doing this because it gives users control over what assets are valid for selection in Motion Matching, something that in previous releases was only possible at the database level, not at the individual asset level.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "This means you can search for the best pose, but also filter the per asset results based on extra conditions like game logic through the Chooser.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "A new Pose Search field in the Choosers manages this complexity, along with integrated debugging from Motion Matching into Choosers.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "This feature is Experimental.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Blendshapes and Sculpting Tools",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26123047,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "Blendshapes and Sculpting Tools",
      "image": {
        "id": 26123047,
        "file_name": "image.png",
        "file_size": 1033488,
        "content_type": "image/png",
        "created_at": "2025-10-07T18:25:14.294+00:00",
        "height": 994,
        "width": 1600,
        "storage_key": "af36a345-b993-4cec-b75a-24d9d291797f",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "af36a345-b993-4cec-b75a-24d9d291797f",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "Bring the flexibility of industry-standard sculpting workflows into Unreal Engine. This is an update on the Skeletal Editor tools that now provides the ability to create and edit morph shapes, bones, and skin weights seamlessly.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Instantly move between placing bones, painting weights, and sculpting blendshapes on a Skeletal Mesh.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "New <b>Morph Target Viewer</b>.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Shapes adapt to changes in mesh topology .",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Equip animators with expressive tools for studio-quality performances.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "World Collisions for Control Rig Physics",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26123049,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "World Collisions for Control Rig Physics",
      "image": {
        "id": 26123049,
        "file_name": "image88.gif",
        "file_size": 2720969,
        "content_type": "image/gif",
        "created_at": "2025-10-07T18:29:43.818+00:00",
        "height": 270,
        "width": 480,
        "storage_key": "f97c5a25-e5a2-4963-a0d8-5008a3f97b32",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "f97c5a25-e5a2-4963-a0d8-5008a3f97b32",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "autoplay": false,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "Bring level collisions into your Control Rig Physics simulations. Use existing world geometry and physics bodies to create believable contacts, pushes, and interactions.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "World collision support in Control Rig physics with one-way interactions.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Use level meshes and physics bodies as colliders.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Drive contact with props, floors, and environment during sim and playback.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Control Rig Dependency View",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26123051,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "Control Rig Dependency View",
      "image": {
        "id": 26123051,
        "file_name": "image35.gif",
        "file_size": 322583,
        "content_type": "image/gif",
        "created_at": "2025-10-07T18:30:49.551+00:00",
        "height": 484,
        "width": 1051,
        "storage_key": "063c4de8-913c-4c35-8a0c-7ef18dc2a37b",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "063c4de8-913c-4c35-8a0c-7ef18dc2a37b",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "autoplay": false,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "Understand how rig elements connect and update over time with a dedicated Dependency View. Visualize relationships between controls, bones, and modules to simplify debugging.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Explore parent-child and metadata relationships at a glance.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Debug rigs and modules with clearer visibility into data flow.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Familiar dependency structure visualization.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "FBIK and Retargeting Performance",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26123052,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "FBIK and Retargeting Performance",
      "image": {
        "id": 26123052,
        "file_name": "image.png",
        "file_size": 408955,
        "content_type": "image/png",
        "created_at": "2025-10-07T18:31:34.401+00:00",
        "height": 643,
        "width": 1480,
        "storage_key": "2014353d-92d7-4b03-b416-701dcfd75e58",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "2014353d-92d7-4b03-b416-701dcfd75e58",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "We added performance improvements and control in Full Body IK and IK Retargeter. This provides a way for you to have performant retargeting at runtime.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Improve posing with FBIK without a heavy performance cost (~20% speed increase).",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Use the stretch limb solver in IK Rig for performant runtime retargets.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Use the FK rotation mode <b>Copy Local</b> for faster rotation transfer per chain.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Enable performance profiling on the retarget stack.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Add a LOD threshold per retarget operator.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Better Foot Contact and Proportioned Retargeting",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26123165,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "Better Foot Contact and Proportioned Retargeting",
      "image": {
        "id": 26123165,
        "file_name": "image64.gif",
        "file_size": 2802725,
        "content_type": "image/gif",
        "created_at": "2025-10-07T19:41:31.869+00:00",
        "height": 322,
        "width": 592,
        "storage_key": "3aa5e368-1f24-490c-b408-9d59fbbf880b",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "3aa5e368-1f24-490c-b408-9d59fbbf880b",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "autoplay": false,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "We improved the IK Retargeter for better handling of ground contact and vast proportioned characters.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "This provides a better transfer of animation across a large variety of characters, so you can do faster art direction. You can now do all the following:",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Define crotch height to prevent the pelvis from crashing to the ground, and maintain vertical motion.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Add the floor constraint to feet to maintain vertical position when close to the floor.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Use the FK translation mode <b>Orient and Scale</b> to transfer bone translation data.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Add the <b>Stretch Chain</b> operator to match the source retarget chain or scale it.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Use the <b>Stretch Limb</b> solver to control squash and stretch of limbs.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Change the pivot of the <b>Source Scale</b> to a different bone for motion capture or gameplay.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Add the <b>Filter Bones</b> operator to remove high frequency noise while retargeting on larger characters.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Spatially Aware Retargeting (Experimental)",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26123266,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "Spatially Aware Retargeting (Experimental)",
      "image": {
        "id": 26123266,
        "file_name": "image51.gif",
        "file_size": 3068156,
        "content_type": "image/gif",
        "created_at": "2025-10-07T20:10:29.605+00:00",
        "height": 322,
        "width": 741,
        "storage_key": "7663d6ad-8c8d-4c1a-908d-2955e0d1bcba",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "7663d6ad-8c8d-4c1a-908d-2955e0d1bcba",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "autoplay": false,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "<b>Spatially Aware Retargeting</b> provides a way for artists to transfer animations between different characters without having to worry about self collision.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "This reduces the need to clean up the animation after a retarget.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "We added a <b>Body Interactions</b> operator to push IK goals out using physics to prevent self collision.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "We added a <b>Relative IK</b> operator to move IK goals relative to contact lengths from the source to the target.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Sequence Validator (Experimental)",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26123268,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "Sequence Validator (Experimental)",
      "image": {
        "id": 26123268,
        "file_name": "image.png",
        "file_size": 232720,
        "content_type": "image/png",
        "created_at": "2025-10-07T20:11:37.493+00:00",
        "height": 643,
        "width": 1480,
        "storage_key": "b65fcd33-ae61-4071-b5b0-286fd0964eb6",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "b65fcd33-ae61-4071-b5b0-286fd0964eb6",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "The <b>Sequence Validator</b> provides a way for cinematic artists to check if there are any content errors in their sequences. This reduces the amount of potential issues that can happen during a production while working in Sequencer.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "Using it, you can:",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Queue multiple sequences and their sequence hierarchies for potential issues.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Check if sections in a sequence are off from the camera cut or playback range.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Check if there are any unassigned bindings or assets on sections.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Check if sections start and/or end on whole frames.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Review results and navigate directly to where the problem is in each sequence.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Ease Curve (Beta) and UX Improvements",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26123270,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "Ease Curve (Beta) and UX Improvements",
      "image": {
        "id": 26123270,
        "file_name": "image.png",
        "file_size": 143346,
        "content_type": "image/png",
        "created_at": "2025-10-07T20:12:17.569+00:00",
        "height": 643,
        "width": 1480,
        "storage_key": "4d7f7b9b-af72-47fc-8f19-fcc586ac17cd",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "4d7f7b9b-af72-47fc-8f19-fcc586ac17cd",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "You can now use the <b>Ease Curve</b> tool from Motion Design in Sequencer. You can enable it using the Ease Curve plugin",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "Additionally, this update brings more cohesive behaviors for better usability so you can iterate faster.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Use the Ease Curve tool to apply and save different Ease Curve presets in Sequencer, Curve Editor, and UMG.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Adjust scale similar to the Details panel in the Sequencer tree using scale lock.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Improved timeline display of sequence hierarchies to display both current and parent sequence time.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Expand Blueprint and Python capabilities for accessing track states (Solo, Mute, Deactivate, Lock, and Pin).",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Improved transform baking to include time warp.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Added new <b>Binding ID</b> and <b>Sequence ID</b> columns to the navigation tool.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "The Ease Curve Tool is currently in Beta.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Dataless Mutable (Experimental)",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26123274,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "Dataless Mutable (Experimental)",
      "image": {
        "id": 26123274,
        "file_name": "image.png",
        "file_size": 823935,
        "content_type": "image/png",
        "created_at": "2025-10-07T20:14:16.216+00:00",
        "height": 890,
        "width": 1600,
        "storage_key": "4fa976ba-2611-4b95-ba7d-bd7b7907a2a7",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "4fa976ba-2611-4b95-ba7d-bd7b7907a2a7",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "Speed up iteration and streamline your workflow with dataless Customizable Objects. Parameter inputs are handled at runtime instead of compile time, giving you faster compiles, better encryption, and smaller packages.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Convert any Customizable Object to dataless.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Enjoy faster compile times with runtime parameter handling.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Use encryption and packaging just like any other UE asset.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Selection Sets",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26123275,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "Selection Sets",
      "image": {
        "id": 26123275,
        "file_name": "image.png",
        "file_size": 131298,
        "content_type": "image/png",
        "created_at": "2025-10-07T20:15:20.671+00:00",
        "height": 331,
        "width": 611,
        "storage_key": "c84ba3d4-3cc3-4931-91b1-325b9ef5cefb",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "c84ba3d4-3cc3-4931-91b1-325b9ef5cefb",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "Animators need quick access to multiple selections throughout their process. This may be multiple controls within one rig, but may also be multiple controls and/or transforms across various assets. Currently animators rely on custom tools like Gui Pickers to do this, so we wanted to build a more native version, similar to popular plugins in other tools.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Animation Mode UX and QOL",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "video",
      "video_id": "V_zI6k7o",
      "provider": "kaltura",
      "caption": "",
      "autoplay": false,
      "loop": false,
      "width": null,
      "video": {
        "height": 642,
        "width": 1480,
        "identifier": "V_zI6k7o"
      },
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "We made improvements to <b>Animation Mode</b>.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "Our <b>Constraints</b>, <b>Space Switching</b>, and <b>Snapper </b>tools are now consolidated into one new <b>Constraint</b> window..",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "Also related to Constraints, we added a few new options to the creation process.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<b>Current Frame</b>: This is the previous standard functionality. It makes an ON key at the current frame and compensates all keys forward.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<b>From Start (new)</b>: Takes the current frame's offset and keys it on the first frame of your shot instead of the current frame.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<b>Infinite (new)</b>: Doesn't add keys. Instead, it applies the current frame's offset to the entire sequence.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "Pose Library, Tween Tools, Constraints, Selection Sets (new) and Animation Layers now toggle their corresponding windows on or off based on their icon state. Their position and size within your layout is also saved.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "You can assign toggling these windows to hotkeys.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    }
  ],
  "excerpt_hash_id": "lYL6",
  "settings": {
    "is_hidden": false
  }
}
```

## MetaHuman

```json
{
  "type": "include",
  "excerpt_id": 2553,
  "excerpt_assignment_id": 2049,
  "blocks": [
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "MetaHuman Creator",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "MetaHuman Creator in Unreal Engine is now available on Linux and macOS, bringing powerful, integrated digital human creation to your preferred platform. If you haven’t already migrated from the MetaHuman Creator web application, follow <a data-document-id=\"3954968\" href=\"https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/en-us/metahuman/metahuman-creator-migration-guide?application_version=5.6\">our migration guide</a> for instructions on how to bring your existing MetaHuman character into Unreal Engine to continue authoring!\n\n",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26244615,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "",
      "image": {
        "id": 26244615,
        "file_name": "image.png",
        "file_size": 1335665,
        "content_type": "image/png",
        "created_at": "2025-11-10T17:00:11.170+00:00",
        "height": 1003,
        "width": 1600,
        "storage_key": "4312e68d-0c2d-47a2-9fb3-bb450bade256",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "4312e68d-0c2d-47a2-9fb3-bb450bade256",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": 600,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "We’ve enhanced the <a data-document-id=\"4596991\" href=\"https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/en-us/metahuman/body-conform-controls\">body mesh conformation process</a> with support for varied poses, and introduced UV-space vertex correspondence between the conform template and model meshes – enabling more accurate results with less effort. The parametric body controls are also now more intuitive, making it easier to stay within human proportions with increased precision and control. ",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "With this release you can automate and batch process nearly all editing and assembly operations for MetaHuman character assets <a data-document-id=\"4596440\" href=\"https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/en-us/metahuman/python-scripting-for-metahuman-creator\">using Python</a> or Blueprints. For the first time, more deeply integrate MetaHuman Creator into your pipeline and creative workflow with an API that supports sculpting, conforming, wardrobe, rigging, and textures, streamlining full character assembly.  Preview edits live in MetaHuman Creator, run them interactively in Unreal Editor or a compute farm for offline processing.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "MetaHumans in Unreal Engine",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26197405,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "",
      "image": {
        "id": 26197405,
        "file_name": "art3.gif",
        "file_size": 3034845,
        "content_type": "image/gif",
        "created_at": "2025-10-28T15:39:12.528+00:00",
        "height": 466,
        "width": 921,
        "storage_key": "ab987bf8-a6d1-4df9-801b-a3045cda52f9",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "ab987bf8-a6d1-4df9-801b-a3045cda52f9",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": 600,
      "autoplay": false,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "The Unreal Engine Groom Plugin now enables finer control in <a data-document-id=\"4596400\" href=\"https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/en-us/metahuman/hair-art-directability\">shaping and animating hair strands</a> with a joint-based workflow. You can now blend simulations with art-directed motion, access full use of skeletal mesh animation workflows, and achieve greater artistic freedom with enhanced selection sets and paint tools. The toolset offers full compatibility for MetaHuman grooms originally created in Houdini.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "MetaHuman Animator",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "Real-time animation from MetaHuman Live Link sources can now be accessed via a Blueprint interface, with <a data-document-id=\"4596401\" href=\"https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/en-us/metahuman/blueprint-examples\">an example graph</a> provided in our documentation.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_table",
      "widths": null,
      "rows": [
        [
          [
            {
              "type": "enhanced_table_header",
              "content": "",
              "settings": {
                "is_hidden": false
              }
            }
          ],
          [
            {
              "type": "enhanced_table_header",
              "content": "",
              "settings": {
                "is_hidden": false
              }
            }
          ]
        ],
        [
          [
            {
              "type": "image",
              "image_id": 26197406,
              "caption": "",
              "alt_text": "",
              "image": {
                "id": 26197406,
                "file_name": "Screenshot 2025-10-28 at 09.48.17.png",
                "file_size": 550959,
                "content_type": "image/png",
                "created_at": "2025-10-28T15:42:23.975+00:00",
                "height": 1073,
                "width": 1190,
                "storage_key": "aa693ef9-1aee-43f1-89e7-774c5d398503",
                "context": "documentation"
              },
              "storage_key": "aa693ef9-1aee-43f1-89e7-774c5d398503",
              "context": "documentation",
              "width": 300,
              "settings": {
                "is_hidden": false
              }
            }
          ],
          [
            {
              "type": "image",
              "image_id": 26197407,
              "caption": "",
              "alt_text": "",
              "image": {
                "id": 26197407,
                "file_name": "Screenshot 2025-10-28 at 09.54.26.png",
                "file_size": 692674,
                "content_type": "image/png",
                "created_at": "2025-10-28T15:42:32.734+00:00",
                "height": 1074,
                "width": 1183,
                "storage_key": "b6f73909-5a48-4787-932c-28140e280bc7",
                "context": "documentation"
              },
              "storage_key": "b6f73909-5a48-4787-932c-28140e280bc7",
              "context": "documentation",
              "width": 300,
              "settings": {
                "is_hidden": false
              }
            }
          ]
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "The workflow for <a data-document-id=\"4776600\" href=\"https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/en-us/metahuman/generate-calibration\">generating calibration data</a> from the checkerboard take – captured using a stereo camera pair – has been improved to provide better automatic frame selection, visualization to aid manual frame selection, and repeatable configurations across multiple takes. ",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "Combined with the new <a data-document-id=\"4776601\" href=\"https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/en-us/metahuman/calibration-diagnostics-tool\">MetaHuman Animator Calibration Diagnostics plugin</a> (experimental) to validate calibration against performance footage to more easily determine if it is the best calibration to use.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "MetaHuman Capture",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "We’ve improved the real-time animation options in the Live Link Face <a href=\"https://apps.apple.com/us/app/live-link-face/id1495370836\">iOS</a> and <a href=\"https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.epicgames.facelink\">Android</a> applications by extending the range of supported cameras.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "With Live Link Face, you can now generate real-time facial animation using an external camera on supported Android devices or iPads (Note: Apple's iOS does not support external cameras at this time).  Video can be recorded from an external camera on iPad only.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "You can also generate animation in realtime by selecting from any on-device camera using Live Link Face on iOS & Android.  Video can be recorded from the on-device camera on iOS only.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "MetaHuman for Houdini",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "video",
      "video_id": "V_3x8YPl",
      "provider": "kaltura",
      "caption": "",
      "autoplay": false,
      "loop": false,
      "width": 500,
      "video": {
        "height": 918,
        "width": 1911,
        "identifier": "V_3x8YPl"
      },
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "The latest <a href=\"https://www.fab.com/listings/7bbdfbb5-5eaf-4aa6-b32b-b8b048ebea25\">MetaHuman for Houdini</a> update brings <a data-document-id=\"4596439\" href=\"https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/en-us/metahuman/parametric-hairstyle-generator\">parametric groom tools</a>, a guide-driven workflow for creating hairstyles using pre-authored data. The toolset includes a follicle generator to handle density and positioning around the hairline that generates guides based on flow, length, and volume parameters, as well as hair strand interpolation and styling for advanced detailing such as clumpiness.  This is a fast and approachable way to block and iterate new hairstyles.  ",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "Meanwhile, for those wanting to learn how to create more advanced hairstyles, we’ve published the new <a href=\"https://www.fab.com/listings/165c4244-482f-4e37-9200-dd782972cdde\">MetaHuman Groom Advanced Kit for Houdini</a> on Fab as an extra learning resource.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "<a href=\"https://www.fab.com/listings/7bbdfbb5-5eaf-4aa6-b32b-b8b048ebea25\">MetaHuman for Houdini</a> is now available for Linux.  ",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "MetaHuman for Maya",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "The latest update to <a href=\"https://www.fab.com/listings/9e3bf55e-d4c3-44fc-a3d4-ec4cb772ec29\">MetaHuman for Maya</a> is available on Linux and introduces support for Maya 2026.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    }
  ],
  "excerpt_hash_id": "G6b6",
  "settings": {
    "is_hidden": false
  }
}
```

## Worldbuilding

```json
{
  "type": "include",
  "excerpt_id": 2518,
  "excerpt_assignment_id": 2050,
  "blocks": [
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Custom HLODs",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26123701,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "Custom HLODs",
      "image": {
        "id": 26123701,
        "file_name": "image.png",
        "file_size": 1324696,
        "content_type": "image/png",
        "created_at": "2025-10-07T21:05:42.738+00:00",
        "height": 463,
        "width": 1480,
        "storage_key": "74d604f2-8453-41e5-8a0a-dce03d7a35a4",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "74d604f2-8453-41e5-8a0a-dce03d7a35a4",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "When using the provided automatic HLOD generation methods, the output may not always meet the project's requirements and is bound to mesh components.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "<b>Custom HLODs</b><b><b></b> support</b> addresses this limitation by giving you the ability to provide your own custom HLOD representations for individual actors or groups of actors.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "You can use the new World Partition Custom HLOD actor class in two ways:",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<b>Inject custom representation directly</b>: You can inject the custom representation as-is into the HLOD runtime partition and optionally use it as input for parent HLOD Layers.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<b>Provide a custom source only</b>: You can use the custom representation as input to the HLOD generation process without adding it to the world itself.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "HLODs Setup UX",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26123710,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "HLODs Setup UX",
      "image": {
        "id": 26123710,
        "file_name": "image.png",
        "file_size": 232452,
        "content_type": "image/png",
        "created_at": "2025-10-07T21:07:12.960+00:00",
        "height": 643,
        "width": 1480,
        "storage_key": "cd130b57-a597-4ee4-8339-4138a2f39fb1",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "cd130b57-a597-4ee4-8339-4138a2f39fb1",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "We reworked <b>World Settings &gt; World Partition</b>'s HLODs layers configuration and HLODs layers assets to improve usability when creating or editing a setup.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    }
  ],
  "excerpt_hash_id": "26yM",
  "settings": {
    "is_hidden": false
  }
}
```

## Procedural Content Generation (PCG)

```json
{
  "type": "include",
  "excerpt_id": 2522,
  "excerpt_assignment_id": 2051,
  "blocks": [
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "PCG Framework is Production Ready",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26124026,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "PCG Framework is Production Ready!",
      "image": {
        "id": 26124026,
        "file_name": "image.png",
        "file_size": 121855,
        "content_type": "image/png",
        "created_at": "2025-10-07T21:21:44.346+00:00",
        "height": 643,
        "width": 1480,
        "storage_key": "c4b2d3f4-88c2-4d92-a86c-109f29ba1b89",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "c4b2d3f4-88c2-4d92-a86c-109f29ba1b89",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "The <b>Procedural Content Generation Framework (PCG)</b> is now Production Ready with the release of UE 5.7.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "While the framework is in continuous development and continues to evolve, we are committed to support a stable environment so all productions can build and rely on this fast and iterative procedural content generation toolset for both runtime and in-editor use cases.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "PCG Editor Mode",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26126268,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "PCG Editor Mode",
      "image": {
        "id": 26126268,
        "file_name": "image42.gif",
        "file_size": 2997361,
        "content_type": "image/gif",
        "created_at": "2025-10-08T15:01:00.251+00:00",
        "height": 278,
        "width": 640,
        "storage_key": "1af90ed6-327f-49df-ae70-7b1ba479472c",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "1af90ed6-327f-49df-ae70-7b1ba479472c",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "autoplay": false,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "A new PCG-specific editor tool mode in Unreal Engine is now available. It offers a library of customizable tools leveraging the PCG framework such as draw spline, paint, or create volume, each with an associated PCG Graph and its parameters available for live editing.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
```
