```json
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "You can use the provided tool-specific PCG graphs as-is or as examples to create and expand the library for your own needs.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "callout",
      "callout_type": "warning",
      "blocks": [
        {
          "type": "paragraph",
          "content": "\n\nIf you used the UE 5.7 preview, your PCG Editor Mode settings might not have the default setup. If your PCG Editor Mode window does not open and instead shows an error, open the <b>Plugins - PCG Editor Mode Settings</b> in the <b>Editor Preferences</b>, then click <b>Reset to Defaults</b> in the upper right corner.\n\n",
          "settings": {
            "is_hidden": false
          }
        }
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Procedural Vegetation Editor",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "video",
      "video_id": "V_aeUMHJ",
      "provider": "kaltura",
      "caption": "",
      "autoplay": false,
      "loop": false,
      "width": null,
      "video": {
        "height": 643,
        "width": 1480,
        "identifier": "V_aeUMHJ"
      },
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "The <b>Procedural Vegetation Editor</b> is a new experimental plugin to create and edit vegetation meshes, supporting Nanite foliage, directly within Unreal Engine.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "In this initial release, procedural vegetation assets you create are edited through the Vegetation Editor, a PCG-based node graph editor. Using the provided importer node and skeletal presets distributed in the plugin's content folder, you can:",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Modify the trees' shape through gravity, carve, and scale operators.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Adjust leaves and branches distribution.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Assign different leaf models.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Export to skeletal mesh or static mesh, including support for Nanite foliage assemblies.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "Create multiple variants through a single node graph or multiple node graphs in order to build a library of unique vegetation meshes for your project.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Assets Workflows",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26126333,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "Assets Workflows",
      "image": {
        "id": 26126333,
        "file_name": "image19.gif",
        "file_size": 2902279,
        "content_type": "image/gif",
        "created_at": "2025-10-08T15:04:46.638+00:00",
        "height": 643,
        "width": 1480,
        "storage_key": "60b901b5-6abf-45e5-9ce5-5d3d0a87bcf7",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "60b901b5-6abf-45e5-9ce5-5d3d0a87bcf7",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "autoplay": false,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "You can now execute PCG graphs as standalone graphs, removing the need for the world context and for the graph to be generated from a PCG component.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "This change adds support for :",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<b>Assets Creation workflows</b>, such as creating meshes using the geometry operations, textures with GPU operations or even generate PCG Data Assets such as attribute set tables by only running a standalone PCG Graph.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<b>Assets Creation tools and plugins built on the PCG framework core</b>, like the new Procedural Vegetation Editor, benefit from its full feature set and future updates.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "To enable PCG graph standalone execution, you can enable a new <b>Is Standalone Graph</b> option is available in each graph's settings. When this option is enabled, you have access to a right click menu <b>Generate Standalone Graph(s)</b> function as well as the possibility to generate, inspect, and debug from the graph editor using a Standalone Graph debug object.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Custom Data Types",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26126382,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "Custom Data Types",
      "image": {
        "id": 26126382,
        "file_name": "image.png",
        "file_size": 329383,
        "content_type": "image/png",
        "created_at": "2025-10-08T15:07:10.297+00:00",
        "height": 643,
        "width": 1480,
        "storage_key": "e13a1f9c-4d88-4dd0-a616-75e432df5b18",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "e13a1f9c-4d88-4dd0-a616-75e432df5b18",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "Since initial release, we hardcoded the list of supported data types in the PCG framework, preventing easy addition and support of new data types when extending the framework or creating interoperations with external plugins.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "In UE 5.7, you can now add support for any custom data type within the types hierarchy and expand the framework, or use the framework core and feature set to build your own tool on top.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "We upgraded the type selector as well to better indicate type relationships and provide for multi-selection.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Polygon2D Data Type and Operators",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26126383,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "Polygon2D Data Type and Operators",
      "image": {
        "id": 26126383,
        "file_name": "image.png",
        "file_size": 377870,
        "content_type": "image/png",
        "created_at": "2025-10-08T15:07:10.362+00:00",
        "height": 643,
        "width": 1480,
        "storage_key": "7b62a031-7df1-4f07-9126-b32b285d966f",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "7b62a031-7df1-4f07-9126-b32b285d966f",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "We added a new <b>Polygon2D</b> data type to the list of supported data types in the PCG Framework.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "Polygon2D data is useful to represent areas as a closed shape which can be converted to surfaces or splines data, for sampling or for specific operations.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "We added a set of new operators to support this new data type:",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<b>Create Polygon 2D</b>: Takes as input point or spline data and converts them into polygon 2D.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "\n<b>Polygon Operations</b>: Allows for polygon-to-polygon operations such\n as intersection, union, and differences. Additionally, polygon 2D can \nbe cut with paths, using splines, to subdivide or isolate shapes. For \nexample, slicing a larger area using spline data in a grid pattern to \ncreate individual city blocks in a city generator graph.\n\n",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "\n<b>Clip Paths</b>: allows to intersect or difference splines with polygon 2d shapes.\n\n",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<b>Offset Polygon</b>: applies an offset to the polygon 2d to make them larger or smaller with proper handling of overlaps.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<b>Create Surface From Polygon2D</b>: will turn the polygon 2D into a surface that can be sampled using the Surface Sampler node to create points over its area. ",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "GPU Fast Geo Interop",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26126431,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "GPU Fast Geo Interop",
      "image": {
        "id": 26126431,
        "file_name": "image26.gif",
        "file_size": 2931493,
        "content_type": "image/gif",
        "created_at": "2025-10-08T15:15:04.927+00:00",
        "height": 405,
        "width": 720,
        "storage_key": "492f3844-b167-4d9d-badb-b8041a39c59a",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "492f3844-b167-4d9d-badb-b8041a39c59a",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "autoplay": false,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "PCG GPU now leverages FastGeometry components to further improve on game thread performances when using the framework to generate and spawn a high density of static meshes, such as ground scatter and grass. It removes the need for any partition actors and creates local PCG components on the fly.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "To benefit from the improvement, enable the PCG FastGeo Interop plugin in your project and set the CVAR <code class=\"inline-code\">pcg.RuntimeGeneration.ISM.ComponentlessPrimitives</code> to 1.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "GPU Operators and Improvements",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26127036,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "GPU Operators and Improvements",
      "image": {
        "id": 26127036,
        "file_name": "image79.gif",
        "file_size": 2761180,
        "content_type": "image/gif",
        "created_at": "2025-10-08T15:57:00.363+00:00",
        "height": 278,
        "width": 640,
        "storage_key": "2424bed6-535f-4a4a-b1fa-65de1d14b91a",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "2424bed6-535f-4a4a-b1fa-65de1d14b91a",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "autoplay": false,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "New additions to the the PCG Framework GPU compute features set:",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<b>Scene Capture</b>: A new node for performing scene captures from the component origin to get and sample the scene color or depth for scattering.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "\n<b>Save Texture to Asset</b>: A new node for baking a PCGTextureData into a UTexture2D asset.\n\n",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "\n<b>Support for Overrides</b>: parameters overrides are now supported on GPU nodes, including Num Elements counts for custom HLSL nodes.\n\n",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "Existing native operators upgraded with GPU compute support:",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<b>Cull Points Outside Actor Bounds</b>",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<b>Transform Points</b>",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "UX Improvements",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26127154,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "UX Improvements",
      "image": {
        "id": 26127154,
        "file_name": "image.png",
        "file_size": 496750,
        "content_type": "image/png",
        "created_at": "2025-10-08T16:00:46.803+00:00",
        "height": 643,
        "width": 1480,
        "storage_key": "a6fc5335-a851-4c0a-b9ae-0a450c50a9fb",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "a6fc5335-a851-4c0a-b9ae-0a450c50a9fb",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "As continued UX improvements when working with PCG, these are latest changes:",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<b>Parameters and Overrides type coloring and validation</b>: Params, override pins, constant output pins and their edges are  all now colored based on their type, matching the Blueprints types colors visual language. When an input can be of any type or is undefined, the pin and edge use white as its display color.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "\n<b>Parameters Min, Max, Units values</b>: You can now configure graph parameters with min/max values and units to display.\n\n",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "\n<b>Data Viewports Controls</b>: We added additional in-graph editor data viewport controls and options to help preview data in specific contexts.\n\n",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Nodes and Operators",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26127373,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "Nodes and Operators",
      "image": {
        "id": 26127373,
        "file_name": "image.png",
        "file_size": 542724,
        "content_type": "image/png",
        "created_at": "2025-10-08T16:05:33.472+00:00",
        "height": 643,
        "width": 1480,
        "storage_key": "ce3257c2-d984-4d53-83ee-ae00c666b349",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "ce3257c2-d984-4d53-83ee-ae00c666b349",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "Unreal Engine 5.7 introduces multiple native PCG elements and new options including:",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<b>Spline Intersection</b>: Finds intersecting splines in 3D and adds control points at intersection or returns the intersection points.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "\n<b>Split Splines</b>: Divides splines into multiple splines based on alpha, distance, key, or control points predicates.\n\n",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "\n<b>Get Segment</b>: Returns a segment index point or spline from a point, spline, or polygon2D.\n\n",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "\n<b>Spawn Spline Mesh Component Overrides</b>: Component class is now \nrespected when spawning the spline mesh, and component property \noverrides in the Spawn Spline Mesh node are supported.\n\n",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "\n<b>New PCG Blueprint Element Classes</b>: added new PCG elements classes\n including range-based overridable functions to support Structure of \nArray format changes introduced in native nodes in 5.6. Using the new \nelements prevents extra data transformation and improves performance \nwhen working with BP PCG Elements.\n\n",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "\n<b>Get Execution Context</b>: Updated with Is Listen Server, Has Authority, and Is Builder context support.\n\n",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "\n<b>Extract Attribute</b>: Retrieves a specific attribute at the given index and outputs it as a new attribute set.\n\n",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "\n<b>Export Selected Attributes</b>: Exports data as Binary or JSON format for external use cases such as ML applications.\n\n",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "\n<b>Attribute Remap</b>: Can now be driven by a curve.\n\n",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "\n<b>Bitwise Operators</b>: Added shift left and shift right.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "\n<b>Boolean Operators</b>: Added NAND, NOR, XNOR, IMPLY, NIMPLY and automatic casting to Boolean from bitwise.\n\n",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "\n<b>Math Operators</b>: New Add Modulo operation was added, useful to offset and re-order with indices.\n\n",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<b>Bounds from Mesh and Points from Mesh</b>: Added support for Skeletal Meshes. ",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Python Interop Plugin",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26128193,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "Python Interop Plugin",
      "image": {
        "id": 26128193,
        "file_name": "image.png",
        "file_size": 201791,
        "content_type": "image/png",
        "created_at": "2025-10-08T17:57:30.453+00:00",
        "height": 643,
        "width": 1480,
        "storage_key": "f38c4059-4332-46c8-b060-54c4b1d15adb",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "f38c4059-4332-46c8-b060-54c4b1d15adb",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "We added a new PCG plugin, which provides a new Execute Python Script node so you can run Python scripts while executing the graphs. You can provide the Python script directly as an input, or run it from a file on disk; you can set the path to the file dynamically through an override soft object path attribute or parameter.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    }
  ],
  "excerpt_hash_id": "7jG9",
  "settings": {
    "is_hidden": false
  }
}
```

## Developer Iteration

```json
{
  "type": "include",
  "excerpt_id": 2521,
  "excerpt_assignment_id": 2052,
  "blocks": [
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Incremental Cooking (Beta)",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "<b>Incremental Cooking</b> is now beta in UE 5.7.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "Incremental Cooking enables you to iterate faster on your target devices by reducing overall cooking time. Cook processes automatically analyze asset changes against output stored in Zen Server, and only cooks new updates made to your project's assets. This works across all native engine assets including Blueprints and World Partition tiles.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "CPP Tools - MSVC 14.44 Support",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "In Unreal Engine 5.7, we updated the default Microsoft Visual C++ compiler to MSVC 14.44.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Build Health Tracking and Visualization (Experimental)",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26128211,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "Build Health Tracking and Visualization (Experimental)",
      "image": {
        "id": 26128211,
        "file_name": "image.png",
        "file_size": 222122,
        "content_type": "image/png",
        "created_at": "2025-10-08T18:02:53.670+00:00",
        "height": 643,
        "width": 1480,
        "storage_key": "7a3307ca-81a8-42b0-ae1d-2500533774cf",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "7a3307ca-81a8-42b0-ae1d-2500533774cf",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "As part of the Horde analytics tooling, we've introduced a new <b>Build Health dashboard</b> experimental feature that gives teams a way to monitor and inspect which BuildGraph steps across the projects change lists are completing as expected, and/or reporting errors.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "This is part of an ongoing development to provide built-in functionality in Horde to help teams better understand the most common cause of build failures, monitor pressure on agent pools and other useful build performance metrics that impede your iteration times.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    }
  ],
  "excerpt_hash_id": "8WZZ",
  "settings": {
    "is_hidden": false
  }
}
```

## Audio

```json
{
  "type": "include",
  "excerpt_id": 2527,
  "excerpt_assignment_id": 2053,
  "blocks": [
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Audio Insights (Beta)",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26128519,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "Audio Insights (Beta)",
      "image": {
        "id": 26128519,
        "file_name": "image50.gif",
        "file_size": 2930982,
        "content_type": "image/gif",
        "created_at": "2025-10-08T18:11:46.462+00:00",
        "height": 801,
        "width": 1479,
        "storage_key": "98c1d942-dec2-478b-8dd4-c0214f18f2be",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "98c1d942-dec2-478b-8dd4-c0214f18f2be",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "autoplay": false,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "<b>Audio Insights</b> is our primary tool for profiling and debugging audio. This targets everyone using audio in Unreal Engine. In this release, in addition to stabilizing the API and architecture, we are shipping a Beta version of Audio Insights. It includes the following additional core features:",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "<b>Monitoring</b>",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Start a live monitoring session without writing data to disk.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Sound designers can observe the operation of a project, both in standalone and editor sessions, for longer periods of time without concern for having enough disk space.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "<b>Editor Support</b>",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Use Audio Insights for in-editor use-cases in addition to PIE.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Audio Insights now react to any audio interaction in the editor.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "<b>Snapshots and Tail Recording</b>",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Writes the last live session data to disk on demand, up to 32 MB.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Audio Insights can detect if a Live session has started, to connect to it",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "<b>Live Loudness Metering (UX Design)</b>",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Provides audio metering in Standalone, specifically for Loudness compliance.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "<b>Scrollable Event History</b>",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Visualize a chronological text list of audio events such as sound start and stop with timestamps.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Selecting a specific event moves the plot and other visualizations to the same timestamp in the data.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "MetaSound Node Configuration",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "video",
      "video_id": "V_Rg4ZQk",
      "provider": "kaltura",
      "caption": "",
      "autoplay": false,
      "loop": false,
      "width": null,
      "video": {
        "height": 802,
        "width": 1291,
        "identifier": "V_Rg4ZQk"
      },
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "<b>MetaSound Node Configuration</b> brings a new layer of expressibility to MetaSounds by making it possible for a node to alter its inputs and outputs. Node Configuration takes advantage of the MetaSound Editor Details panel to provide a pathway for modifying node interfaces and serializing sidecar data usable when a node renders audio.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "In addition to giving sound designers and developers a way to create more nuanced and interactive nodes, Node Configuration provides improved UX. For instance, by cleaning up the node palette and by reducing the number of steps and rewiring complexity for many common MetaSound design tasks.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Audio Subtitle Plugin",
      "level": 2,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26129076,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "Audio Subtitle Plugin",
      "image": {
        "id": 26129076,
        "file_name": "image1.gif",
        "file_size": 3114349,
        "content_type": "image/gif",
        "created_at": "2025-10-08T18:27:28.942+00:00",
        "height": 433,
        "width": 1000,
        "storage_key": "bbbe6a69-edda-477f-8777-614d04692e12",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "bbbe6a69-edda-477f-8777-614d04692e12",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "autoplay": false,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "Audio subtitles are required for accessibility – dialogue systems, closed captioning, and voice chat. In Unreal Engine 5.7 we are further improving the Subtitle Plugin with the following additions:",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Subtitles can now be driven by other SoundBase types in addition to the original SoundWave and independent-timing support in 5.6.  Thus, subtitles can now base their time with respect to SoundCues or the top level of a MetaSound.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "By default, subtitles attached to <code class=\"inline-code\">USoundWave</code> asset userdata are automatically played and stopped using the time the audio starts and stops.  However, there is a use case for using the subtitle duration property. We added an enum to provide a method for users to choose which duration to use.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Multiple subtitles are now supported in the userdata asset.  Rather than using multiple assets for multiple subtitles associated with a given SFX, you now instead have all the subtitles in one asset.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "You can now enable or disable subtitles globally using <code class=\"inline-code\">UEngine::bSubtitlesEnabled</code>.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Audio Waveform Editor",
      "level": 2,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26129134,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "Audio Waveform Editor",
      "image": {
        "id": 26129134,
        "file_name": "image78.gif",
        "file_size": 2723327,
        "content_type": "image/gif",
        "created_at": "2025-10-08T18:30:24.116+00:00",
        "height": 869,
        "width": 1596,
        "storage_key": "3273165c-57be-4949-85c9-2930e3be6345",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "3273165c-57be-4949-85c9-2930e3be6345",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "autoplay": false,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "Often sound designers need to go back and forth from UE to an external tool when working with audio assets. To help their workflow in Unreal Engine 5.7, we added the display of markers and loop regions from imported sound files to the <b>Audio Waveform Editor</b>.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "This includes additional quality of life improvements to expand the wave editor to be sufficiently featured so that sound designers can stay within UE for common audio editing tasks.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Format / Channel Agnostic Types (CAT)",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "Sound designers often need to send and receive multichannel audio data between specific MetaSound nodes of varying formats and channel counts. In UE 5.7, audio programmers can code custom nodes containing input and output references using <b>Channel Agnostic Types (CAT)</b>:",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "The new audio-rate data type provides a way for audio buffers to support multiple channels in one pin type.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Nodes can now support multiple configurations for audio channel formats.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Reduces clutter and complexity in MetaSound Graphs.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Tech Audio Tools Sample",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "Tech Audio’s experience and usage of editor utility can be extremely beneficial to the game audio community. This initiative is to help onboard new developers to UE Audio, make working at scale less daunting, and serve as a starting point for developers to fork from in building their own project-specific tooling.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "A set of blueprint scripting utilities, tools, and graphs to help with working with Unreal Audio technology.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Tech Audio would like to build an exemplar collection of editor utilities and workflows to use internally and showcase externally.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    }
  ],
  "excerpt_hash_id": "j1jA",
  "settings": {
    "is_hidden": false
  }
}
```

## Platform

```json
{
  "type": "include",
  "excerpt_id": 2526,
  "excerpt_assignment_id": 2054,
  "blocks": [
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Android Platform Support",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "We improved workflows for developers building, packaging, and deploying Unreal Engine projects to Android devices.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Deploy on Android 15 with 16kb page support",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Skip manual SDK setup adjustments on macOS.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<code class=\"inline-code\">bDontBundleLibrariesInAPK</code> setting is now enabled by default, ensures libraries are kept external for improved package size and deployment flexibility. ",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Mobile Renderer",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "Unreal Engine 5.7 brings improvements and new features to the <b>Mobile Renderer</b>.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "You can now use the Multipass deferred rendering mode which enables contact shadows and inset shadows.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "In addition, SSAO, SSR and DFAO are now scalability options, which you can toggle at runtime, not needing to be set as project options and re-packaging anymore.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Stereo Layer",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "Stereo layer components are now visible when the X axis points toward the camera. To enable legacy behavior (visible when X points away from the camera) use the console variable:<code class=\"inline-code\"> XR``.StereoLayers.UseLegacyFacing true</code>.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "Emulated stereo layers now support depth and are OpenXR compliant in how they render. You can turn off the new rendering using the console variable: <code class=\"inline-code\">XR``.StereoLayers.RenderEmulatedLayerDepth false</code>.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "OpenXR Application SpaceWarp",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "Application SpaceWarp is Meta's developer-driven optimization technology that can unlock extra computation power for suitable content. In UE 5.7, we implemented support not only for Meta's <code class=\"inline-code\">XR_FB_space_warp</code> extension, but also for OpenXR runtimes that support \n<code class=\"inline-code\">XR_EXT_frame_synthesis</code>\n\n. This technology forces the application to render at half framerate, and synthesize every other frame with an updated head orientation - greatly reducing the load on the GPU.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "Frame synthesis (and SpaceWarp) is controlled with <code class=\"inline-code\">xr.OpenXRFrameSynthesis</code>. You can enable it at runtime, but it requires <code class=\"inline-code\">r.Velocity.DirectlyRenderOpenXRMotionVectors=1</code>.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "OpenXR User Presence",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "We can now return events when a head mounted display is donned or doffed for OpenXR runtimes that support the OpenXR extension <code class=\"inline-code\">XR_EXT_user_presence</code>. While this has been exposed through the <b>VRNotificationsComponent</b> in the past, the new method is more accurate and behaves the same between runtimes.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    }
  ],
  "excerpt_hash_id": "N5Ak",
  "settings": {
    "is_hidden": false
  }
}
```

## Motion Design

```json
{
  "type": "include",
  "excerpt_id": 2524,
  "excerpt_assignment_id": 2055,
  "blocks": [
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Motion Design Production Ready",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26130092,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "Motion Design Production Ready",
      "image": {
        "id": 26130092,
        "file_name": "image100.gif",
        "file_size": 2990512,
        "content_type": "image/gif",
        "created_at": "2025-10-08T19:26:59.286+00:00",
        "height": 643,
        "width": 1480,
        "storage_key": "e81e8e66-3059-4985-8234-17e755934f71",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "e81e8e66-3059-4985-8234-17e755934f71",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "autoplay": false,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "As customers begin to implement Motion Design into their core workflows, the Motion Design team will continue to survey customer feedback and focus on further performance optimization opportunities for the toolset.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "Getting Motion Design to a Production Ready state required the following:",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Harden packaging support.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Refactor Text3D to support rich text and masking capability.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Transition Logic stability and increased ease of use and setup.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Enhance Remote Control functionality by adding interface organizational tools and refactor general UX for accelerated setup time.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Upgrade Scene State based on client feedback.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Create communication between Scene State and Transition Logic.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Ensure Material Designer reliability.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Text3D — Rich Text",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26130110,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "Text3D — Rich Text",
      "image": {
        "id": 26130110,
        "file_name": "image.png",
        "file_size": 331780,
        "content_type": "image/png",
        "created_at": "2025-10-08T19:30:14.413+00:00",
        "height": 643,
        "width": 1480,
        "storage_key": "c3223087-c392-40fe-bd42-d7d30aa0fbb3",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "c3223087-c392-40fe-bd42-d7d30aa0fbb3",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "Give your text layouts a dynamic edge with both pre-configured or dynamically customizable text. Create a Text3D style set using our new dedicated asset where you can create a consistent configuration you can use across your project on any Text3D actor.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "Previously, this sort of layout would require several Text3D actors and an Auto-Follow modifier. In Unreal 5.7, these can be edited in-line, reducing the actor count and even providing a way for you to remote-control the individual text sections.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Supports assigning fonts, colors, sizing,",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Flexible styling tools adapt to any art direction.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Ideal for designs with dynamic UI.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Exposes each section of the text line to remote control for further customization. ",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          },
          {
            "type": "enhanced_list",
            "style": "unordered",
            "items": [
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "This is useful when you want to break apart something like \"Brand New Enriched Text\" into three separate controllers while retaining the style of each word.",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ]
            ],
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Transition Logic Quality of Life",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26130260,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "Transition Logic Quality of Life",
      "image": {
        "id": 26130260,
        "file_name": "image.png",
        "file_size": 112292,
        "content_type": "image/png",
        "created_at": "2025-10-08T19:35:38.258+00:00",
        "height": 643,
        "width": 1480,
        "storage_key": "7d432986-6c5d-47bb-a4fa-1d33ba89cce6",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "7d432986-6c5d-47bb-a4fa-1d33ba89cce6",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "<b>Transition Logic</b> is critical to the broadcast workflow and, based on customer feedback, we reevaluated the steps required to create a level and set up Transition Logic. You can now accomplish all of the steps in the image above by creating a new level and making sure that the <b>Create Transition Logic Default Scene</b> option is enabled, as seen in the top left corner of the image.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "Below are the setup steps that Transition Logic automates when you enable Create Transition Logic Default Scene:",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Create pre-labeled In and Out sequences.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Create pre-labeled Layer Change sequences.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          },
          {
            "type": "enhanced_list",
            "style": "unordered",
            "items": [
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "You can add additional (and pre assigned) Layer Change sequences with new entries in the Sequencer options panel.",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ]
            ],
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Pre-set the sequence settings on those sequences for immediate compatibility.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Create a default layer so that you can get to work immediately and test your design.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Add the Transition Logic Sublayer Modifier to a null actor in your Motion Design Outliner and pre-assign it to the generated In and Out sequences.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "All that is required of you beyond that is to actually animate your content using the generated In, Out, and Change sequences.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Remote Control UX",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26130578,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "Remote Control UX",
      "image": {
        "id": 26130578,
        "file_name": "image.png",
        "file_size": 748715,
        "content_type": "image/png",
        "created_at": "2025-10-08T19:41:16.130+00:00",
        "height": 643,
        "width": 1480,
        "storage_key": "4aa2614b-f35e-4752-85c6-7c5c412d3394",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "4aa2614b-f35e-4752-85c6-7c5c412d3394",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "We added or improved several features that save you time while rigging your designs.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "<b>New Controllers</b>",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Category",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          },
          {
            "type": "enhanced_list",
            "style": "unordered",
            "items": [
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "Add categories to separate your controllers into groups.",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ],
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "Single select or multi-select your controllers and drag them into the categories.",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ]
            ],
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "String List",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          },
          {
            "type": "enhanced_list",
            "style": "unordered",
            "items": [
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "Curate a named dropdown list that you can link to your conditional behaviors to enhance the end-user experience.",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ],
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "Previously, solving this would require entering integers where \"1 = Blue, 2= Red, 3= Yellow,\" now you can name the dropdown entries Red, Blue, Yellow.",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ]
            ],
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Events",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          },
          {
            "type": "enhanced_list",
            "style": "unordered",
            "items": [
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "Create a button that can play Motion Design sequences, trigger an On Modify property, or trigger a broadcast event.",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ]
            ],
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "<b>Path Behavior</b>",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Previously, each Path Behavior entry could only address a single actor. There is now a list that appears under the Actions panel where you can add as many actors as you want to effect, reducing the need for multiple Path Behaviors and making it easier to troubleshoot your setup.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Reordering the path list was previously not possible; now, you can use the left side drag handle to reorder as necessary.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "The Path Preview now informs you if the path that you're routing to is valid or not with a green checkmark.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "<b>UI Enhancement</b>",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "We added a link icon to the left-side properties panel to show whether an exposed property is assigned to a controller.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Scene State",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26130622,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "",
      "image": {
        "id": 26130622,
        "file_name": "image75.gif",
        "file_size": 2921130,
        "content_type": "image/gif",
        "created_at": "2025-10-08T19:44:12.227+00:00",
        "height": 643,
        "width": 1480,
        "storage_key": "181a0970-ad86-41d6-a59e-fd9b6ec958e6",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "181a0970-ad86-41d6-a59e-fd9b6ec958e6",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "autoplay": false,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "<b>Scene State</b> in UE 5.7 received several new features and UX improvements that greatly improve user control.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "State Machine Organization",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          },
          {
            "type": "enhanced_list",
            "style": "unordered",
            "items": [
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "Reorder State Machines by dragging and dropping.",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ],
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "Add categories.",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ]
            ],
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Added Setter Tasks as part of property references.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          },
          {
            "type": "enhanced_list",
            "style": "unordered",
            "items": [
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "Datatypes include: Boolean, Float, Int, String, Text.",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                },
                {
                  "type": "enhanced_list",
                  "style": "unordered",
                  "items": [
                    [
                      {
                        "type": "paragraph",
                        "content": "Each datatype has unique functionality.",
                        "settings": {
                          "is_hidden": false
                        }
                      }
                    ]
                  ],
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ]
            ],
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Property Functions",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          },
          {
            "type": "enhanced_list",
            "style": "unordered",
            "items": [
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "Concatenate String",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ],
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "Text to String",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ],
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "Name to String",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ],
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "Integer to String",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ],
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "Float to String",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ]
            ],
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Spawn Actors and Spawn Actors to Tickers.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Data Link",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "<b>Data Link </b>received two much-requested updates:",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Push Functionality, providing a method for constant detection of changing values from feeds to trigger updates.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Interaction with with Scene State to provide a method for scrolling tickers and other living elements.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    }
  ],
  "excerpt_hash_id": "rBdZ",
  "settings": {
    "is_hidden": false
  }
}
```

## Production Rendering Pipeline

```json
{
  "type": "include",
  "excerpt_id": 2531,
  "excerpt_assignment_id": 2056,
  "blocks": [
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Material Parameter Collection Modifier",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26133532,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "Material Parameter Collection Modifier",
      "image": {
        "id": 26133532,
        "file_name": "image.png",
        "file_size": 537108,
        "content_type": "image/png",
        "created_at": "2025-10-09T14:54:04.574+00:00",
        "height": 643,
        "width": 1480,
        "storage_key": "ed6055ab-461f-48ba-ba4f-316ee9a104a0",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "ed6055ab-461f-48ba-ba4f-316ee9a104a0",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "<b>Material parameter collections</b> are powerful assets that can drive multiple properties on multiple materials at once. Creative looks and effects within a scene are often driven dynamically by material parameter collections.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "The <b>Material Parameter Collection Modifier</b> provides a way for you to set and override parameter values on a per-render layer basis in <b>Movie Render Graph</b>. Using the same level and level sequence, you can render shots with different parameter values on each render layer. You can also make parameters into graph variables and expose them as overrides in both the <b>Quick Render</b> and the <b>Movie Render Queue</b> windows.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Burn In Per-Output Type",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26133535,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "Burn In Per-Output Type",
      "image": {
        "id": 26133535,
        "file_name": "image.png",
        "file_size": 1161882,
        "content_type": "image/png",
        "created_at": "2025-10-09T14:54:06.100+00:00",
        "height": 643,
        "width": 1480,
        "storage_key": "088892bb-64ac-44df-83f8-6b81863d01a8",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "088892bb-64ac-44df-83f8-6b81863d01a8",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "The ability to render multiple output types in the same job means Movie Render Graph can provide both final and review media simultaneously. Typically, Burn In is desirable for some but not all of the output types in a job. To account for this, Movie Render Graph now sets <b>Burn In Per-Output Type</b> instead of by using a separate node in a branch. This makes it much simpler to, for example, have clean EXR renders but include Burn In on MP4s.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "File Naming Control",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26133530,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "File Naming Control",
      "image": {
        "id": 26133530,
        "file_name": "image.png",
        "file_size": 85528,
        "content_type": "image/png",
        "created_at": "2025-10-09T14:54:04.231+00:00",
        "height": 643,
        "width": 1480,
        "storage_key": "5447ba21-8fe8-47c6-acec-8c2a49216299",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "5447ba21-8fe8-47c6-acec-8c2a49216299",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "Strong naming conventions on render output are a foundational building block of efficient pipelines in all industries. With this in mind, Movie Render Graph offers much more robust <b>File Naming Control</b> to better support studios' specific naming requirements natively, without the need for additional pipeline scripting. Key improvements include:",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<b>Custom Naming Tokens</b> defined in the Cinematic Assembly tools are supported in all name fields.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "You can customize {renderer_name} (for example, \"Deferred\") and {renderer_sub_name} (for example, \"beauty\"), and conflicts are resolved per-branch rather than for the entire graph, offering more flexibility.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<b>Burn In</b> no longer forces {renderer_name} on the beauty render when it is not composited.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<b>Post Process Material</b> passes can use a separate naming convention from the beauty render if desired.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "EXR Metadata Per Render Layer",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26133534,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "EXR Metadata Per Render Layer",
      "image": {
        "id": 26133534,
        "file_name": "image.png",
        "file_size": 465477,
        "content_type": "image/png",
        "created_at": "2025-10-09T14:54:05.075+00:00",
        "height": 643,
        "width": 1480,
        "storage_key": "befb0db0-740b-4615-b953-b16f27b80fa8",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "befb0db0-740b-4615-b953-b16f27b80fa8",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "<b>EXR Metadata</b> now follows a convention that reliably separates key/value pairs on a per-render layer basis and avoids naming conflicts. Based on this convention, metadata from Unreal Engine renders can be queried directly in post-compositing applications such as Nuke without the need for additional pipeline tooling.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    }
  ],
  "excerpt_hash_id": "LX48",
  "settings": {
    "is_hidden": false
  }
}
```

## Content Pipeline

```json
{
  "type": "include",
  "excerpt_id": 2528,
  "excerpt_assignment_id": 2057,
  "blocks": [
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Customize USD Asset Import with Interchange (Experimental)",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26134134,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "Customize USD Asset Import with Interchange (Experimental)",
      "image": {
        "id": 26134134,
        "file_name": "image.png",
        "file_size": 33294,
        "content_type": "image/png",
        "created_at": "2025-10-09T15:41:16.489+00:00",
        "height": 643,
        "width": 1480,
        "storage_key": "adeab5cc-c991-4d9c-a8ea-71329b503275",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "adeab5cc-c991-4d9c-a8ea-71329b503275",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "USD is a versatile scene description file format with a growing adoption across the 3D industry.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "We added USD to the list of file formats supported by the Interchange framework in UE 5.5. Content Creators benefit from more control over the import process and are now able to add custom processing steps for asset import of USD files, similar to the Interchange capabilities already available with other formats such as FBX.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "The support for USD through Interchange is considered Experimental and limited to import of assets. In UE 5.7 we added support for:",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Groom",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Dome Lights (not the DomeLight_1 schema)",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Audio (UsdMediaSpatialAudio)",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Point Instance",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "FBX Level Import with Interchange",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26134135,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "FBX Level Import with Interchange",
      "image": {
        "id": 26134135,
        "file_name": "image.png",
        "file_size": 151881,
        "content_type": "image/png",
        "created_at": "2025-10-09T15:41:17.103+00:00",
        "height": 510,
        "width": 1600,
        "storage_key": "b1a46672-e749-4c82-92f3-d37700f88bd1",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "b1a46672-e749-4c82-92f3-d37700f88bd1",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "In an effort to align the UI/UX for importing different file formats into the engine, the FBX level import process is available through the Interchange Framework. This is now the default path to import an FBX into a level. You can still fall back to the legacy behavior with the help of a CVar.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
```
