```json
      "content": "The main change comes from the use of the same import dialog as for import in the content folder. Legacy functionalities such as removing assets or actors from import, or using a different set of options to import, can now be performed through custom Interchange pipelines.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    }
  ],
  "excerpt_hash_id": "EgmO",
  "settings": {
    "is_hidden": false
  }
}
```

## Editor and UI Systems

```json
{
  "type": "include",
  "excerpt_id": 2520,
  "excerpt_assignment_id": 2058,
  "blocks": [
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "AI Assistant (Experimental)",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26134160,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "AI Assistant (Experimental)",
      "image": {
        "id": 26134160,
        "file_name": "image17.gif",
        "file_size": 3141735,
        "content_type": "image/gif",
        "created_at": "2025-10-09T16:38:50.387+00:00",
        "height": 643,
        "width": 1480,
        "storage_key": "4ce4c1e5-8bab-4d2b-9802-bf4d69a9d4c5",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "4ce4c1e5-8bab-4d2b-9802-bf4d69a9d4c5",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "autoplay": false,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "The <b>AI Assistant</b> gives you a faster way to get answers without leaving Unreal Engine. By sliding out a dedicated panel in the Editor, you can ask questions, generate code, or follow step-by-step guidance, all without switching to a browser.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "You save time and stay in flow. Instead of breaking concentration to search online, you can get instant help, examples, and troubleshooting tips right inside the editor.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "<b>What you can do:</b>",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Offer step-by-step instructions for tasks.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Get guidance and tips directly in context.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Troubleshoot development topics with AI help.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Generate code examples to accelerate workflows.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Reference past exchanges with chat history.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Copy and paste answers into your project.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "The AI Assistant is powered by the same specially-trained AI already available in UEFN and on the Developer Community—tailored to understand Unreal Engine and help you move faster.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Home Screen",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26134159,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "Unreal Engine Home Screen",
      "image": {
        "id": 26134159,
        "file_name": "image.png",
        "file_size": 844610,
        "content_type": "image/png",
        "created_at": "2025-10-09T16:37:53.709+00:00",
        "height": 643,
        "width": 1480,
        "storage_key": "784b0f86-4a84-4eb2-afcf-4bc373e1e006",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "784b0f86-4a84-4eb2-afcf-4bc373e1e006",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "The <b>Home Screen</b> helps you get oriented in Unreal Engine by providing a welcoming starting point. It highlights essential resources such as tutorials, documentation, and forums.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "You get an easier, more guided onboarding experience. Instead of facing an empty editor, you have clear navigation to learning resources, news, and support, all in one place—helping you start projects with confidence and direction.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "<b>What you can do:</b>",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Access the latest news and updates.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Navigate directly to Getting Started resources.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Open tutorials and documentation.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Visit the forums and release notes.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Launch links externally in Chrome.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Turn off the Home Screen if you prefer not to see it every time.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "The Home Screen serves as a one-stop onboarding hub, making Unreal Engine more approachable from the very first launch.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Orthographic Clipping Interactions",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26134163,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "Orthographic Clipping Interactions",
      "image": {
        "id": 26134163,
        "file_name": "image99.gif",
        "file_size": 3110605,
        "content_type": "image/gif",
        "created_at": "2025-10-09T16:42:05.880+00:00",
        "height": 643,
        "width": 1480,
        "storage_key": "2e5f454f-2fee-4882-ad48-d1635770e749",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "2e5f454f-2fee-4882-ad48-d1635770e749",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "autoplay": false,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "Orthographic viewports now provide more reliable and flexible control. In earlier versions, clipping planes were often unusable, making it difficult to adjust what was visible in the scene.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "You gain precise control over what you see in orthographic views, making it easier to focus on exactly the geometry you need.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "<b>What you can do:</b>",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Adjust near and far clipping planes with greater accuracy.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Use new visual controls directly in the viewport.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Customize visibility in orthographic views to match your workflow.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "This improvement, fixed in UE 5.7, makes orthographic workflows more practical and predictable.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Improved Save and Delete Dialog Boxes",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26134161,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "Improved Save and Delete Dialog Boxes",
      "image": {
        "id": 26134161,
        "file_name": "image.png",
        "file_size": 101804,
        "content_type": "image/jpeg",
        "created_at": "2025-10-09T16:41:40.657+00:00",
        "height": 643,
        "width": 1480,
        "storage_key": "fe818cc4-3a97-4455-819a-1c95218b7e23",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "fe818cc4-3a97-4455-819a-1c95218b7e23",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "We improved the <b>Save </b>and <b>Delete </b>dialog boxes in Unreal Engine to make asset management smoother. Common actions now work as expected, reducing unnecessary clicks and interruptions to your workflow.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "You spend less time wrestling with repetitive prompts and more time focusing on your project.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "<b>What you can do:</b>",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Use the Enter key in the Asset Deletion popup (previously unavailable).",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Delete multiple assets without repeatedly clicking Accept with the mouse.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Rely on more consistent and intuitive dialog box interactions.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "These improvements arrive in UE 5.7 and streamline everyday workflows by fixing long-standing pain points.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Improved Window Dragging Interactions",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26134204,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "Improved Window Dragging Interactions",
      "image": {
        "id": 26134204,
        "file_name": "image15.gif",
        "file_size": 1675367,
        "content_type": "image/gif",
        "created_at": "2025-10-09T16:54:51.153+00:00",
        "height": 446,
        "width": 1024,
        "storage_key": "610fc139-afb8-402f-ba35-d3641a897467",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "610fc139-afb8-402f-ba35-d3641a897467",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "autoplay": false,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "Dragging windows inside the editor is now more intuitive and less disruptive. In previous versions, clicking and dragging on any header bar would undock the entire Level Editor from fullscreen, often by accident.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "You avoid frustrating misclicks and keep your workspace stable, so you can stay focused on building.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "<b>What you can do:</b>",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Drag windows and panels without undocking the full Level Editor.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Prevent accidental breaks in fullscreen workflows.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Enjoy more predictable and user-friendly window interactions.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "This improvement, fixed in UE 5.7, addresses a common frustration and makes window management more reliable.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Improved Blueprint Editor Duplicate Interactions",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26134206,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "Improved Blueprint Editor Duplicate Interactions",
      "image": {
        "id": 26134206,
        "file_name": "image25.gif",
        "file_size": 2087615,
        "content_type": "image/gif",
        "created_at": "2025-10-09T16:56:37.277+00:00",
        "height": 445,
        "width": 1024,
        "storage_key": "940f5768-67c5-480f-b047-522f7f5c28a1",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "940f5768-67c5-480f-b047-522f7f5c28a1",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "autoplay": false,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "Duplicating items in the Blueprint Editor is now smoother and less disruptive. In previous versions, duplicating would clear your selection, forcing you to manually reselect items in the tree view.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "You keep momentum while editing, avoiding extra clicks and rework when duplicating elements.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "<b>What you can do:</b>",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Duplicate items in the Blueprint Editor without losing selection.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Maintain active selections when working in the tree view.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Reduce repetitive steps when duplicating multiple items.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "This improvement, fixed in UE 5.7, makes Blueprint editing more efficient and frustration-free.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Geometry Inspection Viewport Modes",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26134208,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "Geometry Inspection Viewport Modes",
      "image": {
        "id": 26134208,
        "file_name": "image7.gif",
        "file_size": 2650214,
        "content_type": "image/gif",
        "created_at": "2025-10-09T16:57:37.187+00:00",
        "height": 514,
        "width": 1183,
        "storage_key": "0f83f10e-23e4-4219-acb7-b82f24dac15d",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "0f83f10e-23e4-4219-acb7-b82f24dac15d",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "autoplay": false,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "The new <b>Model Inspection Viewport Modes</b> in Unreal Engine provide simplified, non-photorealistic visualization options. These modes prioritize geometric clarity over realism, removing lighting and shadows when inspecting mechanical or complex 3D models:",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<b>Clay</b>",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<b>Zebra</b>",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<b>Front/Back Face</b>",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<b>Random Color</b>",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "User Asset Tags",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26134209,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "User Asset Tags",
      "image": {
        "id": 26134209,
        "file_name": "image.png",
        "file_size": 152895,
        "content_type": "image/png",
        "created_at": "2025-10-09T16:58:45.840+00:00",
        "height": 721,
        "width": 1155,
        "storage_key": "e7bcdf07-b951-4e43-ba6d-c5ffd4dc747f",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "e7bcdf07-b951-4e43-ba6d-c5ffd4dc747f",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "The <b>User Asset Tags</b> system provides a way to add arbitrary tags to your assets. It supports local favorites as well as project-wide tags, and you can extend it to suggest additional tags. These tags serve as the foundation for additional systems such as the <b>Tagged Asset Browser</b> used in Niagara. Select assets you want to tag, and use Ctrl + T or select <b>Manage Tags</b> in the context menu.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Tagged Asset Browser (Niagara)",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26144105,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "Tagged Asset Browser (Niagara) 1",
      "image": {
        "id": 26144105,
        "file_name": "image.png",
        "file_size": 351932,
        "content_type": "image/png",
        "created_at": "2025-10-14T17:38:30.097+00:00",
        "height": 792,
        "width": 1408,
        "storage_key": "617aef02-fc21-4706-bb3b-b67a247bda5f",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "617aef02-fc21-4706-bb3b-b67a247bda5f",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26144106,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "Tagged Asset Browser (Niagara) 2",
      "image": {
        "id": 26144106,
        "file_name": "image.png",
        "file_size": 354484,
        "content_type": "image/png",
        "created_at": "2025-10-14T17:38:30.210+00:00",
        "height": 504,
        "width": 1600,
        "storage_key": "0d51dcd7-060b-495f-862c-04b55e6e8ce1",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "0d51dcd7-060b-495f-862c-04b55e6e8ce1",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "An update of the previous revamp of the <b>Niagara Asset Browser</b>. Now generalized, it lays the foundation for asset factories that want to adopt the same browser. You can extend it by creating a new <b>Tagged Asset Browser Configuration</b> asset, defining your own sections and listed tags. This way it becomes possible for your projects to have their own sections and tags, or swap out the main configuration asset entirely in the Project Settings under Niagara Editor.\n\n",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "For help upgrading existing Niagara systems and emitters to use the new Tagged Asset Browser Configuration asset, see <a data-document-id=\"4596094\" href=\"https://dev.epicgames.com/documentation/en-us/unreal-engine/upgrading-niagara-tags-in-unreal-engine\">Upgrading Niagara Tags</a>.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    }
  ],
  "excerpt_hash_id": "9knx",
  "settings": {
    "is_hidden": false
  }
}
```

## Framework

```json
{
  "type": "include",
  "excerpt_id": 2529,
  "excerpt_assignment_id": 2059,
  "blocks": [
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "State Tree: Rewind Debugger",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26134446,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "State Tree: Rewind Debugger",
      "image": {
        "id": 26134446,
        "file_name": "image.png",
        "file_size": 615646,
        "content_type": "image/png",
        "created_at": "2025-10-09T20:23:29.594+00:00",
        "height": 1439,
        "width": 1183,
        "storage_key": "212f9a88-54f3-41c4-9574-fa901043abe8",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "212f9a88-54f3-41c4-9574-fa901043abe8",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "We’re introducing a new <b>Rewind Debugger track</b> for State Tree that brings an enhanced, owner- and instance-oriented debugging experience directly into the Rewind Debugger. This integration provides deeper context around when and how behavior logic is executed, all while aligned with gameplay and animation timelines.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "The new track includes a dedicated Details panel with a compact tree view, making it easier to visualize and navigate complex logic setups, including nested subtrees and linked assets. This improves usability when working with larger or modular State Trees.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "While this integration marks a shift toward a more unified debugging workflow, the original State Tree debugger remains available as an option. You can continue using the legacy interface if you prefer it for some use cases.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "State Tree Runtime",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26134447,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "State Tree Runtime",
      "image": {
        "id": 26134447,
        "file_name": "image.png",
        "file_size": 265831,
        "content_type": "image/png",
        "created_at": "2025-10-09T20:23:30.471+00:00",
        "height": 733,
        "width": 1600,
        "storage_key": "ff962086-24a6-49a2-a196-cbd3751766ac",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "ff962086-24a6-49a2-a196-cbd3751766ac",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "This update brings a range of runtime-level enhancements that improve the correctness and performance of State Tree-based logic.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<b>Live Property Binding</b>: Nodes can now push value updates to bound outputs dynamically during execution using Output Properties, resolving issues with outdated or static data in tick workflows.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<b>ExecutionRuntimeData</b>: Nodes can now declare optional, long-lived data that persists for the life of the State Tree instance, useful for holding execution state across transitions or ticks without relying on static globals.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<b>Memory Optimization for Static Bindings</b>: Delegate dispatchers, listeners, and property references are now stored outside of instance data, reducing per-node memory overhead.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "We implemented the following features but did not enable them by default to avoid disrupting existing data and default behaviors. However, you can opt in to use them if desired.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<b>Re-Enter State Behavior</b>: Added support for re-entering the same state and restarting tasks, providing you with a way to implement restarting logic more easily.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<b>Instance Data Refactor</b>: Conditions and transitions now use scoped instanced data, preventing data leakage between sessions and making possible both temporary and persistent usage patterns.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Mass Debugger",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26134449,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "Mass Debugger",
      "image": {
        "id": 26134449,
        "file_name": "image.png",
        "file_size": 142939,
        "content_type": "image/png",
        "created_at": "2025-10-09T20:23:30.603+00:00",
        "height": 660,
        "width": 1253,
        "storage_key": "c22d5186-0b55-44a8-95a7-129e963e084f",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "c22d5186-0b55-44a8-95a7-129e963e084f",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "We’ve introduced a powerful new <b>entity search system</b> integrated directly into the Mass debugger, giving developers a new UI for crafting and executing MassEntity queries. The interface mirrors Mass's C++ query API 1:1, allowing for precise filtering of entities using the same criteria and requirements as hard-coded queries.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "You can now create, save, load, and share custom queries, and immediately view the results in the <b>Entities tab</b>. This improves how fast and efficiently developers inspect and validate Mass state at runtime.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "Additionally, we substantially upgraded the <b>breakpoint system</b>, offering more control and visibility when debugging Mass processors and fragment writes.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Autogen NavLink Multiple Jump Config",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26134452,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "Autogen NavLink Multiple Jump Config",
      "image": {
        "id": 26134452,
        "file_name": "image.png",
        "file_size": 1321558,
        "content_type": "image/png",
        "created_at": "2025-10-09T20:23:31.621+00:00",
        "height": 778,
        "width": 1214,
        "storage_key": "81a2caf5-4e5a-4303-a36f-4fe62b041b22",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "81a2caf5-4e5a-4303-a36f-4fe62b041b22",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "We’ve expanded automatic navigation link generation to support <b>multiple jump configurations</b> per navmesh generation pass. Previously, you could only use a single jump configuration, which often required overly broad tolerances to handle diverse link types (for example. jump ups, downs, and across). This also meant that all generated links shared the same area class and link proxy class, limiting flexibility.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "With this update, you can now define multiple distinct configurations, each processed in its own validation pass. This enables more targeted and meaningful control. As an example, generating jump-downs and jump-overs separately, each with different distance thresholds, area types, or proxy behavior. It makes possible more nuanced movement setups while reducing overgeneration or unintended overlaps.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "To ensure backward compatibility, we migrated any existing single jump configuration as the first entry in the new multi-configuration setup.pState Tree Usability",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26134448,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "State Tree Usability 1",
      "image": {
        "id": 26134448,
        "file_name": "image.png",
        "file_size": 36488,
        "content_type": "image/png",
        "created_at": "2025-10-09T20:23:30.555+00:00",
        "height": 397,
        "width": 1048,
        "storage_key": "d22a1b1e-52e6-4eed-a01b-900f52f7c151",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "d22a1b1e-52e6-4eed-a01b-900f52f7c151",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26134450,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "State Tree Usability 2",
      "image": {
        "id": 26134450,
        "file_name": "image.png",
        "file_size": 41130,
        "content_type": "image/png",
        "created_at": "2025-10-09T20:23:30.574+00:00",
        "height": 402,
        "width": 874,
        "storage_key": "0567b2f8-7ffa-4bc5-9413-5c85460fcae8",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "0567b2f8-7ffa-4bc5-9413-5c85460fcae8",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "We're continuing to enhance the State Tree editor experience with several quality-of-life upgrades aimed at improving clarity and usability:",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "You can now <b>copy and paste tasks, conditions, and transitions</b> between states, even across different State Tree assets. This makes it much easier to reuse logic and maintain consistent behavior across large behaviors.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "We've also added the ability to <b>copy and paste individual property bindings</b>. Bound and unbound properties are handled intelligently:",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          },
          {
            "type": "enhanced_list",
            "style": "unordered",
            "items": [
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "Bound properties replace their bindings if compatible.",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ],
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "Unbound properties carry over values if a value is pasted, or carry over the binding if a compatible binding is pasted.",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ],
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "Pasting a value onto a bound property is disabled.",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ],
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "Pasting an incompatible type, or if the source data is inaccessible, will trigger a validation error.",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ]
            ],
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "You can <b>hide properties within nodes and instance data based on schema</b>, reducing visual noise and improving focus.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Mass Runtime",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26134451,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "Mass Runtime",
      "image": {
        "id": 26134451,
        "file_name": "image.png",
        "file_size": 222329,
        "content_type": "image/png",
        "created_at": "2025-10-09T20:23:31.293+00:00",
        "height": 192,
        "width": 464,
        "storage_key": "3dc031b5-39a0-4a3c-8023-2bb3a269983b",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "3dc031b5-39a0-4a3c-8023-2bb3a269983b",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "This update includes a series of low-level optimizations and architectural refinements to the Mass framework, aimed at improving overall performance, memory efficiency, and system stability in real-time scenarios.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "The most notable addition is <b>Processor Time-Slicing</b>, which means long-running processors can be split across multiple frames. This helps reduce performance spikes and enables better distribution of heavy workloads, particularly useful in large-scale or simulation-heavy environments.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Iris (Beta)",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "With Iris moving out of Experimental and into Beta, we’ve focused on solidifying key APIs and improving usability, maintainability, and performance across the networking stack. This release includes several internal design changes and quality-of-life improvements for developers working directly with Iris replication systems.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<b>Actor Factory Overrides</b>: Actors can now specify custom <code class=\"inline-code\">NetActorFactory</code> implementations using <code class=\"inline-code\">StartReplicationParams</code>, providing more flexibility in replication behavior at runtime. This enables advanced replication setups without relying on global overrides.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<b>Cleaner API Boundaries</b>: We removed the Iris <code class=\"inline-code\">UReplicationBridge</code> base class to reduce virtual call overhead and simplify code navigation. Most Iris systems already depend on <code class=\"inline-code\">UObjectReplicationBridge</code> directly, so this change streamlines the inheritance model and avoids unnecessary indirection.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<b>Improved Replication Lifecycle Naming</b>: We clarified the <code class=\"inline-code\">BeginReplication</code> API:",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          },
          {
            "type": "enhanced_list",
            "style": "unordered",
            "items": [
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "The non-virtual function is now <code class=\"inline-code\">StartActorReplication</code>, clearly indicating its purpose as the entry point to start replication.",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ],
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "We renamed the virtual callback to <code class=\"inline-code\">OnBeginReplication</code>, separating lifecycle behavior from system calls.",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ]
            ],
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<b>Better Consistency Across Iris and Legacy Networking</b>: <code class=\"inline-code\">OnBeginReplication</code> and <code class=\"inline-code\">EndReplication</code> are now consistently called regardless of whether Iris is enabled, ensuring behavior parity and making the transition between networking systems more seamless.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<b>Seamless Travel Support</b>: Iris now supports Unreal Engine’s seamless travel feature, ensuring that replicated actors and states are handled correctly across world transitions. This is a key requirement for shipping game features that rely on persistent player data or transitions between large streaming worlds.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "<br>",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    }
  ],
  "excerpt_hash_id": "0NzW",
  "settings": {
    "is_hidden": false
  }
}
```

## Media

```json
{
  "type": "include",
  "excerpt_id": 2530,
  "excerpt_assignment_id": 2060,
  "blocks": [
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Media Viewer (Beta)",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26135570,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "Media Viewer (Beta)",
      "image": {
        "id": 26135570,
        "file_name": "media-viewer.gif",
        "file_size": 3136258,
        "content_type": "image/gif",
        "created_at": "2025-10-09T21:10:50.494+00:00",
        "height": 433,
        "width": 1000,
        "storage_key": "9765fd5f-d4ab-4f8c-ac27-6a42159ffa5d",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "9765fd5f-d4ab-4f8c-ac27-6a42159ffa5d",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "autoplay": false,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "Often animators need to align their animations against existing reference footage. In 5.6 we added the capability to dock media (images, media textures, video files, live viewports) next to where animation work is done. Two AB banks in either horizontal or vertical configurations help compare two sets of assets. Zoom and pan controls can be used to further align content. In UE 5.7, the <b>Media Viewer</b> plugin is now in Beta as it includes the following workflow and usability improvements:",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Enhanced controls such as rotation and zoom controls using ALT + RMB.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Ability to bookmark and quickly recall asset configurations with hot keys.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "The lock button now also mirrors the playback controls and timeline while in dual Player mode.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    }
  ],
  "excerpt_hash_id": "onaL",
  "settings": {
    "is_hidden": false
  }
}
```

## Simulation and VFX

```json
{
  "type": "include",
  "excerpt_id": 2525,
  "excerpt_assignment_id": 2061,
  "blocks": [
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Chaos Destruction",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26138674,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "Chaos Destruction",
      "image": {
        "id": 26138674,
        "file_name": "image21.gif",
        "file_size": 2745381,
        "content_type": "image/gif",
        "created_at": "2025-10-10T16:52:09.112+00:00",
        "height": 480,
        "width": 854,
        "storage_key": "1bfac456-0d5a-42c2-8259-c6722ec80730",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "1bfac456-0d5a-42c2-8259-c6722ec80730",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "autoplay": false,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "The <b>Chaos Destruction</b> system is a suite of tools designed to achieve cinematic-quality levels of destruction in real-time. In addition to its great-looking visuals, the system is optimized for performance, granting artists and designers more control over content creation and the fracturing process through an intuitive, non-linear workflow.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "<b>UE 5.7 updates include:</b>",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<b>Reset State</b>: You can now reset the state of the geometry collection component using C++, Blueprint, or live in the editor while simulating.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<b>Export to Skeletal Mesh</b>: You can now export geometry collection assets to a skeletal mesh asset.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<b>Leaf Sub-trace</b>: After tracing against a geometry collection, you can now get the exact leaf cone from a line trace. When unbroken, the original line trace against the component returns the root bone. With this new function, you can now get the exact bone the trace is hitting. This makes possible things like setting decals on the right bones or even anchoring individual pieces at runtime. ",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Chaos Dataflow Editor",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "video",
      "video_id": "V_X7q1YU",
      "provider": "kaltura",
      "caption": "",
      "autoplay": false,
      "loop": false,
      "width": null,
      "video": {
        "height": 2160,
        "width": 3840,
        "identifier": "V_X7q1YU"
      },
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "The <b>Dataflow </b>graph makes possible the procedural generation of assets within a node-based environment. The implementation of the graph uses a pull-based evaluation, where the nodes of the graph generate a cached output state based on the evaluation of its inputs. Dataflow assets are used to assist in the procedural authoring and setup of simulation systems.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "Chaos Cloth, Destruction, Flesh, and Hair use the unified Dataflow node-based toolset.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "<b>UE 5.7 updates include:</b>",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "New timeline and simulation controls.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "A new toolbar.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "A redesigned Paint Weight Map tool (to match the Skeletal Mesh Editor).",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "A new Edit Skin Weight tool (to match the Skeletal Mesh Editor).",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "A new Edit Skeleton Bones tool.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Many quality of life Improvements.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Chaos Visual Debugger",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26138677,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "Chaos Visual Debugger",
      "image": {
        "id": 26138677,
        "file_name": "chaos-visual-debugger.gif",
        "file_size": 9162236,
        "content_type": "image/gif",
        "created_at": "2025-10-10T16:58:19.091+00:00",
        "height": 506,
        "width": 900,
        "storage_key": "8ea085fd-d82e-45cf-947c-a80b39b6a91c",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "8ea085fd-d82e-45cf-947c-a80b39b6a91c",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "autoplay": false,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "The <b>Chaos Visual Debugger (CVD)</b> is a versatile tool designed to record and play back the state of a physics simulation during gameplay. It supports recording of all active Rigid and RBAN solvers, including particle states and collision data. CVD is accessible as an editor tool in Unreal Engine and offers extensive customization options, such as choosing specific data channels to record, either through the UI or using console commands.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "The tool also features live debugging capabilities, giving you real-time inspection of physics data during simulation. Additionally, CVD includes a variety of visualization options, such as particle state, collision data, and scene queries, to assist you in analyzing and troubleshooting physics simulations effectively.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "<b>UE 5.7 updates include:</b>",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<b>Object Name Improvements</b> : In this release, we introduced a new feature called <b>particle metadata</b>.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          },
          {
            "type": "enhanced_list",
            "style": "unordered",
            "items": [
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "This new metadata replaces the old long \"Debug Name\" string.",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ],
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "CVD now shows the name of the actor owning the physics body name, and breaks it down in different fields (Level, Component, and Bone index).",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ],
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "In addition, we introduced a <b>Find in Content Browser</b> button that leverages this new metadata and provides a way for you to find the Blueprint a specific body comes from in a single click.",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ]
            ],
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "\n<b>Performance improvements</b>: In this release, we continued working on improving CVD's performance.\n\n",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          },
          {
            "type": "enhanced_list",
            "style": "unordered",
            "items": [
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "CVD files with a large amount of data (specifically over 2 GB of collision geometry data) now loads up to 30% faster.",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ],
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "Reduced a stall of +10 seconds that could happen while loading the first frame of complex scenes (+93.000 objects),to ~3 seconds.",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ]
            ],
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "This release also includes bug fixes and QoL improvements.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Chaos Cloth",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "video",
      "video_id": "V_aIPXwI",
      "provider": "kaltura",
      "caption": "",
      "autoplay": false,
      "loop": false,
      "width": null,
      "video": {
        "height": 600,
        "width": 1107,
        "identifier": "V_aIPXwI"
      },
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "As cloth simulation and complex clothing become more widely requested in-engine, our legacy skeletal mesh cloth editor and workflow cannot easily adjust to future upgrades necessary to support those needs. Fashion and most character work has almost exclusively pivoted to using DCCs which specialize in panel-based clothing construction. In order to grow with these external DCC's we continue to develop our cloth editor in order to ingest and edit panel setups while refining the cloth setup workflow.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "Chaos Cloth for Unreal Engine 5.7 includes the continued development of a Beta Cloth Editor. ",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "<b>UE 5.7 updates include:</b>",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<b>Skeletal mesh clothing data integration:</b>",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          },
          {
            "type": "enhanced_list",
            "style": "unordered",
            "items": [
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "You can now add cloth assets and outfit assets as skeletal mesh clothing data.",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ],
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "The skeletal mesh render sections can then be deformed by the cloth simulation setup inside these assets dataflow graph, removing the need for an additional cloth component.",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ]
            ],
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<b>New reference space options:</b>",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          },
          {
            "type": "enhanced_list",
            "style": "unordered",
            "items": [
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "Use the new ReferenceBone dataflow node to explicitly set the ReferenceBone rather than use the auto-computed one.",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ],
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "Use the new Local Damping space option to solve in ReferenceBone space instead of using center of mass.",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ]
            ],
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<b>Optimizations</b>: We implemented optimizations to the cloth game thread and interactor tick.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<b>Accessory meshes (Experimental):</b>",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          },
          {
            "type": "enhanced_list",
            "style": "unordered",
            "items": [
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "We added additional accessory meshes and updated the backstop constraint to be able to use this accessory mesh.",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ],
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "Currently, you can create accessory meshes by converting an existing sim mesh collection into an accessory mesh. If Imported Vertex IDs exist on both the source and target collections, you can use these to match up vertex indices that were reordered as part of a SKM import.",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ]
            ],
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Chaos Core",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26138678,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "Chaos Core",
      "image": {
        "id": 26138678,
        "file_name": "image.png",
        "file_size": 166640,
        "content_type": "image/png",
        "created_at": "2025-10-10T17:54:00.537+00:00",
        "height": 1600,
        "width": 1600,
        "storage_key": "04f0626c-e927-4cf5-9e9c-12f98b7ea326",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "04f0626c-e927-4cf5-9e9c-12f98b7ea326",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "<b>Chaos Physics</b> is continually undergoing updates and optimizations in order to give artists, developers, and programmers better simulation results. Rigid Body dynamics is the core technology that most game physics rely upon. As we make progress towards bigger and bigger physics-enabled and physics-based worlds, we need to make sure this core system scales and can handle those use cases. Like rigid body dynamics, queries are a core technology that is relied upon by a wide range of systems inside Unreal Engine, and like rigid body dynamics, we need to make sure that query performance scales to very large dynamic worlds.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "<b>UE 5.7 updates include:</b>",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<b>Simulation performance improvements:</b>",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          },
          {
            "type": "enhanced_list",
            "style": "unordered",
            "items": [
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "More parallel simulation stages.",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ],
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "We added the Experimental Partial Sleeping feature for improved performance of large unstructured piles.",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ]
            ],
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<b>Query performance improvements:</b>",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          },
          {
            "type": "enhanced_list",
            "style": "unordered",
            "items": [
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "Improvements to sphere and capsule narrow-phase.",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ],
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "Improvements to the general query layer transform and scale handling.",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ]
            ],
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<b>Chaos Visual Debugger (CVD):</b>",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          },
          {
            "type": "enhanced_list",
            "style": "unordered",
            "items": [
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "Improved loading speed for scenes with large actor counts and complex geometry.",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ],
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "QoL changes and runtime-debugger consistency improvements.",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ]
            ],
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<b>Dataflow physics assets:</b>",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          },
          {
            "type": "enhanced_list",
            "style": "unordered",
            "items": [
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "Initial support for the creation of physics assets using dataflow graphs.",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ]
            ],
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Many QoL changes and fixes across the simulation featureset.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Chaos Caching",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26138680,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "Chaos Caching",
      "image": {
        "id": 26138680,
        "file_name": "image14.gif",
        "file_size": 2217606,
        "content_type": "image/gif",
        "created_at": "2025-10-10T18:01:44.174+00:00",
        "height": 480,
        "width": 854,
        "storage_key": "103174f9-ca16-4c44-923e-3daae4d6f323",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "103174f9-ca16-4c44-923e-3daae4d6f323",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "autoplay": false,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "In Unreal Engine, <b>Chaos Cache</b> is a system within the Chaos Destruction framework that records and reuses complex destruction simulations, significantly improving performance and enabling consistent, predictable results for features like short films. You manage caching using a <b>Chaos Cache Manager</b>, which records physics data into a <b>Chaos Cache Collection</b> asset.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "This cached data can then be played back at runtime to display the destruction without recalculating the entire simulation, making complex scenes more efficient and less demanding on hardware.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "<b>UE 5.7 updates include:</b>",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<b>Geometry Collection Root Proxy Mesh Support:</b> Chaos cache now properly supports root proxy meshes and will switch to use them depending on the state of destruction of the geometry collection asset being cached and replayed.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<b>Automatic Increment of Cache Assets:</b> Chaos cache manager now has an option to automatically create a new asset each time it starts a new recording session.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<b>Session Start/Stop Controls:</b> You can now control when the caching session starts and stops. By default, the cache session starts when the Begin Play event is triggered for the cache actor (usually when starting PIE or Simulation). An option to disable this behaviour is available, requiring you to trigger the start of the session using the start and stop Blueprint functions.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<b>Observed Component Functions:</b> Blueprint functions are now available to add, update, remove, or clear observed components of a specific ChaosActorManager.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<b>Major Bug Fixes:</b>",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          },
          {
            "type": "enhanced_list",
            "style": "unordered",
            "items": [
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "When recording a cached Geometry Collection twice during the same session, events now always record properly the second time.",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ],
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "When replaying a cache in Play Mode, the Niagara Data Interface for Chaos Destruction now always triggers trailing events during the replay as intended.",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ]
            ],
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Chaos Hair",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "video",
      "video_id": "V_xnJmI5",
      "provider": "kaltura",
      "caption": "",
      "autoplay": false,
      "loop": false,
      "width": null,
      "video": {
        "height": 584,
        "width": 1080,
        "identifier": "V_xnJmI5"
      },
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "The <b>Hair and Groom</b> initiative focuses on building a robust simulation pipeline for realistic hair behavior using Dataflow, skeletal meshes, and guide splines. The system allows for animation-driven simulations, blending between kinematic and simulated strands, and advanced authoring features like guide deformers and painting tools.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "Groom Asset procedural workflows benefit from the UE 5.7 update to the general Dataflow Editor improvement. We improved the corresponding nodes to provide for a more flexible setup:",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<b>Selection-aware nodes:</b>",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          },
          {
            "type": "enhanced_list",
            "style": "unordered",
            "items": [
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "Most nodes do take a selection, which results in a more controlled and targeted setup.",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ],
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "You can use existing selection nodes for other assets (Destruction, Flesh) to create selection sets and manipulate them procedurally.",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ]
            ],
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "<b>Performance:</b>",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          },
          {
            "type": "enhanced_list",
            "style": "unordered",
            "items": [
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "Groom assets with complex geometry generate the geometry for the construction viewport faster, and now cache the information to avoid recomputing it.",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ],
              [
                {
                  "type": "paragraph",
                  "content": "This applies to other collection-based nodes across Dataflow Editors.",
                  "settings": {
                    "is_hidden": false
                  }
                }
              ]
            ],
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "<b>UE 5.7 updates include:</b>",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Simulate hair with guide splines driven by animation and physics.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Blend between animated and simulated states for realism and control.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Support for painting attributes, skin weighting, and guide swapping.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Chaos Fluids",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26138682,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "Chaos Fluids",
      "image": {
        "id": 26138682,
        "file_name": "chaos-fluids.gif",
        "file_size": 2967015,
        "content_type": "image/gif",
        "created_at": "2025-10-10T18:06:36.761+00:00",
        "height": 283,
        "width": 590,
        "storage_key": "eb9bee35-2ff6-4831-9735-2f16dfabb925",
        "context": "documentation"
      },
      "storage_key": "eb9bee35-2ff6-4831-9735-2f16dfabb925",
      "context": "documentation",
      "width": null,
      "autoplay": false,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "The <b>Fluids</b> project continues to expand Unreal Engine’s water simulation capabilities, with targeted efforts in UE 5.7 aimed at the Advanced Water plugin, mainly improvements for baked river simulations.",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "paragraph",
      "content": "<b>UE 5.7 updates include: </b>",
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "enhanced_list",
      "style": "unordered",
      "items": [
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Now supports virtual textures.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Improved surface quality (less spikes artifacts).",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ],
        [
          {
            "type": "paragraph",
            "content": "Various bug fixes and performance improvements.",
            "settings": {
              "is_hidden": false
            }
          }
        ]
      ],
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    }
  ],
  "excerpt_hash_id": "dedv",
  "settings": {
    "is_hidden": false
  }
}
```

## Virtual Production

```json
{
  "type": "include",
  "excerpt_id": 2523,
  "excerpt_assignment_id": 2062,
  "blocks": [
    {
      "type": "header",
      "content": "Live Link — Data Preview",
      "level": 3,
      "settings": {
        "is_hidden": false
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "image",
      "image_id": 26138734,
      "caption": "",
      "alt_text": "Live Link Data Preview",
      "image": {
        "id": 26138734,
        "file_name": "live-link-data-preview.gif",
        "file_size": 2752157,
        "content_type": "image/gif",
```
